afador: The Afador is a cross between the elegantAfghan Houndand friendlyLabrador Retrieverdog breeds. Loyal, energetic, and affectionate, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents. These dogs possess an exceptional combination of traits that make them outstanding companions.
affenhuahua: The Affenhuahua is a mixed breed dog–a cross between theChihuahuaandAffenpinscherdog breeds. Petite, sassy, and highly energetic, these pups inherited some of the best traits from both of their parents.
affenpinscher: The Affenpinscher is a small toy breed from Germany, originally bred for pest control in homes, stables, and shops. While highly effective at catching mice, they later became popular as companion pets. A terrier-like breed, the Affenpinscher’s name reflects its distinctive appearance: “Affe” means monkey in German, and “Pinscher” means terrier. However, their charming looks are just one of the many traits that make this breed a beloved companion.
afghan hound: The Afghan Hound is a medium-to-large-sized hound breed first bred by the Bedouin people of Afghanistan to hunt gazelle and other small game in the harsh terrain of the Afghan mountains. Later, Afghan Hounds were also used as war dogs. Of course, this beloved breed found their way into historical art and literature.recognized by their unique aesthetic, silky fur, and piercing eyes, they’re undeniably striking. Beyond appearance, Afghan Hounds are also incredibly intelligent and highly trainable. An ancientdog breedwith a history dating back thousands of years, Afghan Hounds have a reputation for being one of the most expensive dog breeds in the world.
airedale terrier: The Airedale Terrier, commonly called the “King of Terriers,” is the largest of all terrier breeds. Airedales are loyal and loving companions, and they make great family pets. They possesses athleticism and are highly trainable, leading them to excel in a variety of dog sports, including obedience, agility, and tracking.
akbash: The Akbash is alarge working breed first used as a livestock guardian in the plains and mountains of Turkey. Sometimes confused with theGreat Pyrenees, the Akbash also commands attention with their strikingwhite coat and confident demeanor. Highly formidable, Akbash dogs actively excel asprotectors of flocks and herds, fearlessly protecting their charges from predators. Their vigilant nature makes them an integral part of farm life. With an acute awareness of their surroundings, Akbash dogs constantly monitor the perimeter, deterring potential threats with their authoritative presence.
akita: The Akita, also known as the Japanese Akita, the Akita Inu, or the Japanese Akita Inu, is a large-sized working breed from Japan. They are known for their loyalty, courage, and independence. They are typically white, brown, or brindle with a thick, double coat that is weather-resistant. Originally bred for hunting large game such as bears and boars, Akitas were also used as guard dogs and were prized for their loyalty and courage.
alaskan klee kai: Small, smart, and energetic, the Alaskan Klee Kai is a relatively new breed. These little dogs have awolf-like appearanceand are sometimes mistaken for miniature Husky. Even the name “Klee Kai” comes from an Inuit term meaning “small dog.” While these little dogs are small, weighing only 16 to 22 pounds, they pack a serious punch of personality.
alaskan malamute: The Alaskan Malamute, bold, friendly, and hardworking, originates from the rugged landscapes of Alaska. With strength, endurance, and unwavering loyalty, these large fluffy dogs were an asset to indigenous people in the north. Belonging to the Mahlemuts of Alaska, Malamutes hold a significant place in the history of the Arctic. They thrived in cold climates, thanks to their thick coat. The double coat also provides insulation against freezing temperatures and comes in various colors. This includes shades of gray, black, white, and copper. It probably goes without saying, butthese pups shed a lot.
american bulldog: The American Bulldog is a medium-to-large-sized breed of dog that originated in the United States. They are known for their stocky build, muscular body, and distinctive “bulldog” head. Friendly, loyal, and affectionate, these dogs make wonderful family pets. They are also known for being good watchdogs and are protective of their families. While the American Bulldog is stocky and muscular, they are also agile. They were first bred for chasing down stray cattle and helping with farm work.
american english coonhound: The American English Coonhound, also known as the Redtick Coonhound, is a large-sized Hound breed and a descendant of theEnglish Foxhound. One of six Coonhound dog breeds, Redtick Coonhounds are known for their speed, endurance, and loud voice in the field. The American English Coonhound is also beloved as a sporting and household companion. It’s also worth noting that these pups will bark and bay at home. This may make them a poor choice if you’ve got nearby neighbors. They are known for their intelligent, energetic, affectionate, and lively temperaments.
american eskimo dog: The American Eskimo Dog, affectionately called the “Eskie,” is a friendly companion breed from Germany. A German Spitz breed, these dogs lost popularity following World War II. In a marketing move, they were renamed the American Eskimo Dog, likely due to their similar appearance to theSamoyed, another fluffy white breed originating in Siberia who later made their way to the Arctic regions and Alaska. You can usually spot Eskie dogs by their small size, fluffy white coats, and curly tails. While the American Eskimo Dog’s coat is usually pure white, they can also have biscuit-colored markings.
american foxhound: The American Foxhound, a breed known for its grace and endurance, originated in the United States. Bred primarily forhunting, the foxhound possesses a keen sense of smell and boundless energy, making them exceptional hunters and tracking companions.. They are also friendly, affectionate, and gentle, making them wonderful family dogs.
american leopard hound: The American Leopard Hound, also known as the American Leopard Cur, is an intelligent and hardydogthat boasts a rich history intertwined with the arrival of Spanish conquistadors. These medium to large-sized hounds trace their lineage back to various European breeds, resulting in a versatilehunting dogprized for their keen tracking abilities and athletic prowess.
american pit bull terrier: The American Pit Bull Terrier is a muscular, short-coated companion dog from a long line of working breed ancestors traced back to England. Originally bred to “bait” bulls in the fighting pits, the breed evolved into all-around farm dogs and later moved into the house to become “nanny dogs” because they were so gentle around children. The American Pit Bull Terrier’s name is often shortened to Pit Bull; however, “Pit Bull” is not an official breed but an umbrella term that refers to a group of breeds with similar physical characteristics. There are different types of Pit Bulls, the five most common being the American Pit Bull Terrier, theAmerican Staffordshire Terrier, theStaffordshire Bull Terrier, theBull Terrier, and theAmerican Bully.
american pugabull: The American Pugabull is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theAmerican Bull DogandPugdog breeds. Medium in size, alert, and loyal, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
american staffordshire terrier: The American Staffordshire Terrier, also known as the AmStaff, is a muscular, short-coated breed from the United States. The breed was developed in the 1800s when the Staffordshire Bull Terrier was brought to America and later bred as a larger version. In time, this breed was ultimately given distinction from theStaffordshire Bull Terrier. The American Staffordshire Terrier may be somewhat imposing in appearance, but they’re known for their loving temperament and are very affectionate with human family members, particularly children. They are also intelligent and eager to please, which makes them highly trainable. To that end, their intelligence means they need adequate mental stimulation. If they don’t get it, they’ll put those strong jaws to use and chew anything out of boredom.
american water spaniel: The American Water Spaniel, a distinct and versatile breed native to the United States, is prized for its exceptional hunting and retrieving abilities, especially in waterfowl environments. Medium-sized and compact, this breed is known for its curly or wavy brown coat and expressive eyes. With a friendly and eager-to-please disposition, American Water Spaniels make superb family pets and devoted companions. Intelligent and adaptabe, these charming pups excel in various roles, from hunting to agility competitions. This breed’s enduring popularity is a testament to its affectionate personality and its knack for adapting to both rural and urban living environments, provided it receives the exercise and mental stimulation it craves.
anatolian shepherd dog: The Anatolian Shepherd Dog is a native of Turkey, where they were bred as a shepherd’s companion andlivestock guardian. They were created with specific traits to resemble the size and color of livestock they defended so predators wouldn’t detect them among the flock. Sometimes called the Anatolian Karabash Dog, they’re a fiercely loyal guard dog and a large, impressivedog breed, frequently weighing 120 to 150 pounds at maturity.
appenzeller sennenhunde: The Appenzeller Sennenhunde, also known as the Appenzeller Mountain Dog, is a medium-sized working breed that originated as an all-around farm dog that would herd livestock, guard the farm, and pull carts in their native Switzerland. Today’s Appenzeller still has the energy, smarts, and self-confidence that makes for valuable working dogs — but they’re anything but low-maintenance.
auggie: The Auggie is an adorable and spirited hybrid who takes some of the most desirable traits from theirAustralian ShepherdandCorgiparents and combines them into one fun-loving, portable package. With a compact yet sturdy build, these pups typically stand 10-15 inches tall at the shoulder once fully grown and weigh 15-35 pounds. Their coats can vary, with some exhibiting the longer, thicker fur of the Aussie parent while others have the Corgi’s shorter but still dense double coat. Coat colors range widely too, including red merle, blue merle, black, and tan points.
aussiedoodle: The Aussiedoodle, also called the Aussiepoo, is a popular cross between anAustralian Shepherdand aPoodle. Fans of the breed love these pups for their intelligence, friendly disposition, and striking appearance. Typically, the Aussiepoo inherits the Poodle’s allergy-friendly coat, which is low-shedding and comes in various colors. As with any hypoallergenic dog breed, the possibility of shedding and allergic reactions to those with sensitivities is still possible. Aussiedoodles come in several sizes — toy, mini, and standard — depending on thePoodle parentused in the breeding.
aussiepom: The Aussiepom is a lively hybrid breed that blends the intelligent and tirelessAustralian Shepherdwith the spirited and portablePomeranian. These playful canines have a plush coat and a graceful frame, making them an ideal choice for active families and individuals alike.
australian cattle dog: The Australian Cattle Dog, also known as a Blue Heeler or Red Heeler, depending on their coat color, is an extremely intelligent, active, and sturdy dog breed. These dogs are fast! And we’re talkingfast, fast! Originally developed by Australian settlers to handle herds of cattle on expansive ranches, they are still used as herding dogs today.
australian kelpie: The Australian Kelpie, often referred to as the Kelpie, is a highly regarded and versatile herdingdog breed. As their name would indicate, these herding dogs hail from Australia. Breed enthusiasts prize these hardworking dogs for their agility, intelligence, and natural instinct to control livestock. While similar to theWorking Kelpie, the Australian Kelpie is classified as a separate breed.
australian retriever: The Australian Retriever is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theAustralian ShepherdandGolden Retrieverdog breeds. Loyal, intelligent, and friendly, these pups inherited some of the best traits from both of their parents.
australian shepherd: The Australian Shepherd is a medium-sized herding breed from the United States known for their colorful coats, intelligence, and friendly personality. Although the breed originated in the United States, their name reflects their ancestral roots, which are traced back to Europe via Australia. Initially utilized as herding dogs, Aussies still possess strong herding instincts today. American ranchers favored Australian Shepherds for their exceptional herding capabilities, and their popularity soared when they began appearing in rodeos.
australian shepherd husky: The Australian Shepherd Husky is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theAustralian ShepherdandSiberian Huskydog breeds. These active dogs are protective and loyal and inherited some of the best traits from both of their parents.
australian shepherd lab mix: The Australian Shepherd Lab Mix is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theAustralian Shepherdand theLabrador Retrieverdog breeds. Medium in size, energetic, and loyal, these pups inherited some amazing traits from both of their parents.
australian shepherd pit bull mix: The Australian Shepherd Pit Bull Mix is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theAustralian ShepherdandPit Bulldog breeds. Energetic, loyal, and quick to learn new things, these pups inherited some of the best traits from both of their parents.
australian terrier: The Australian Terrier, a small and spirited breed, hails from Australia and is characterized by its distinctive rough double coat that comes in shades of blue and tan. Despite its diminutive size, this terrier is known for its confident and feisty personality. It’s an alert and vigilant watchdog, always ready to alert its family to any potential threats. Australian Terriers are also remarkably loyal and affectionate with their owners, making them loving companions. Their intelligence and curiosity make them quick learners, but their independent streak may require consistent training. With their compact size and adaptable nature, Australian Terriers are suitable for both city and country living, provided they receive regular exercise and mental stimulation.
azawakh: The Azawakh is a sighthound breed originating from West Africa. These elegant and athletic dogs are known for their impressive speed and agility. The nomadic Tuareg people of the Sahara desert historically bred them for hunting game, including gazelles and other swift prey. Today, the Azawakh continues to excel in variousdog sportsand is gaining popularity as a companion and show dog. These remarkable dogs have a statuesque build, with a deep chest and long, slender legs that enable them to cover vast distances with ease. They stand about 23 to 29 inches tall at the shoulder and weigh between 35 to 55 pounds. Their short coat further enhances their distinctive appearance, coming in various colors and patterns, such as red, blue, black, brindle, and white.
barbet: The Barbet, also known as the French Water Dog, is a medium-sized sporting breed first bred to retrieve waterfowl. They possess a distinctive, hypoallergenic curly coat in multiple colors, including grey, black, fawn, or brown, usually with white markings. Their fuzzy coat often hangs over their eyes, giving them the impression of an oversized teddy bear. In addition to their unique coat, the Barbet’s physical characteristics include webbed feet and a well-muscled body to help them easily move through the water. While avid swimmers, they are also talented agility competitors in their native France.
basenji: The Basenji is an ancient hunting dog originally from Central Africa and categorized as both a sighthound and a scenthound. They stand between 16 and 24 inches tall at the shoulder and weigh between 22 and 24 pounds. Their coat is short and smooth and is typically red, black, brindle, or fawn. Basenjis have a long, narrow head with a pointed muzzle and erect ears. They have a deep chest and a short, muscular body. Basenjis are known for making unique barkless vocalizations. They yodel, which is a high-pitched, somewhat musical sound. They also bark but are much quieter than other dogs.
bassador: The Bassador is a mixed breed dog–a cross between theBasset Houndand theLabrador Retrieverdog breeds. Loyal, sometimes stubborn, and always sniffing around, these pups inherited some of the best traits from both of their parents.
basset fauve de bretagne: The Basset Fauve de Bretagne dog, originating from France, is an oldbreedwith a unique appearance and historical significance. With a lineage tracing back to the 19th century, these dogs were selectively bred from larger French hounds to create a compact yet robust hunter, specifically tailored for navigating the challenging terrains of Brittany. Known for their keen sense of smell, they were originally employed for hunting small game, showcasing determination and agility in their pursuit.
basset hound: The Basset Hound, originally bred in France as ahunting companion, is a medium-sizedhound dogwith a long, low body and short legs. They are known for their distinctive features, including their long, droopy ears, wrinkled foreheads, and sad-looking eyes. These gentle and affectionate dogs make great family pets. They are also relatively low-maintenance, requiring only moderate exercise.
basset retriever: The Basset Retriever is a medium-sized cross between theBasset HoundandGolden Retrieverdog breeds. With a friendly, affectionate, and intelligent temperament, Basset Retrievers inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents. You can find these mixed-breed dogs in shelters and breed-specific rescues, so remember to always adopt!
bavarian mountain scent hound: The Bavarian Mountain Scent Hound, also known as the Bavarian Mountain Hound, originated in Germany. Developed in the 19th century, this stunning breed came from the Bavaria region in the southeast of Germany. Breeders specifically bred this dog for its exceptional scenting abilities, making it an idealcompanion for tracking and huntingin challenging mountain terrains.
beabull: The Beabull is a medium-sized designer cross between theBeagleand theEnglish Bulldogbreeds. Loyal, curious, and loving, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents. The Beabull doesn’t go by many other names, though some people might just call them Beagle-Bulldog mixes.
beagle: The Beagle is a small-sized scent hound from England who was primarily bred to track small game such as rabbits and foxes. With a short, smooth coat in various colors, including white, black, brown, and tan, the Beagle is known for their happy-go-lucky spirit. It’s rare to see these little dogs without their tails wagging. The Beagle temperament is characterized by friendliness, playfulness, and intelligence. Their incredible sense of smell can lead them astray if they catch a scent during outdoor walks, so owners should ensure their dogs are on a leash to prevent them from wandering too far. They are still used for hunting today but are also popular as companion dogs. This breed is relatively low-maintenance, only requiring regular exercise and grooming.
beaglier: The Beaglier or Beagalier is a cross between theBeagleandCavalier King Charles Spanielknown for their affectionate and playful temperament. and Compact, energetic, and loyal, these pups inherited some of their parents’ best qualities. Despite their unfortunate status as a designer breed, you may find these pups in shelters and rescues. So remember to adopt! Don’t shop!
bearded collie: Scottish breeders developed the Bearded Colliedog breedtoherdsheep and cattle in any weather or terrain. Even though these are purebred dogs, you may find them in the care of shelters or rescue groups.
bedlington terrier: The Bedlington Terrierdog breedoriginated as a killer of vermin and hunter’s sidekick. While their unique appearance and dapper lamb-like coat may make them seem a little less ferocious, these dogs excelled as small hunters. Today, Bedlingtons primarily shine as companions and in the show ring. Although they still have excellent hunting instincts, a keen sense of smell, and the will to go to ground, they are rarely used in the field.
belgian malinois: The Belgian Malinois is a large-sized herding breed and one of the four Belgian Shepherd breeds. The Malinois, pronounced “mal-in-wa,” may sometimes be mistaken for aGerman Shepherd, and share similar characteristics. However, they are smaller, more energetic, and tend to bond more closely with one individual rather than their entire family, like the German Shepherd. Like the other Belgian Shepherds, Belgian Malinois are high-energy dogs who thrive on physical activity. They possess remarkable stamina and require regular exercise to stimulate them mentally and physically. This Belgian dog breed is renowned for their intelligence, agility, and athletic abilities.
belgian sheepdog: The Belgian Sheepdog, also called the Groenendael, is one of the four Belgian Shepherd herding breed varieties, along with theBelgian Laekenois, theBelgian Malinois, and theBelgian Tervuren. The Belgian Sheepdog is sometimes called the Chien de Berger Belge or Belgien Shepherd. However, it’s important to note that these names refer to the entire herding breed category, not the distinct breed.
belgian tervuren: Originating in Belgium in the late 19th century, the Belgian Tervurendog breedis often considered to be the most elegant of the four Belgian sheepdogs. They’re intelligent and athletic, making them a versatile performer in any number of activities, including their original job,herding.
berger picard: Also known as the Picardy Shepherd, the Berger Picarddog breedis considered one of the oldest of the French sheepdogs. The breed can be recognized by its shaggy coat, trademark erect, wedge-shaped head and expressive, dark almond-shaped eyes. This sheepdog’s origin begins centuries ago in the Picardy region of northern France. Farmers primarily used the breed for herding livestock, including sheep and cattle.
bernedoodle: The Bernedoodle is a cross between theBernese Mountain Dogand thePoodle.“Invented” in 2003, Bernedoodles and their smaller hybrid relatives, theMini Bernedoodle, have exploded in popularity ever since. This versatile mixed breed comes in three size variations. These sizes are Mini, Medium, and Standard. This makes them adaptable to various living situations. A full-grown Bernedoodle is often medium-to-large-sized. It can reach up to 90 pounds in weight. At the shoulder, it stands 29 inches tall. Known for their hypoallergenic qualities, Bernedoodles often have wavy or curly coats that need regular grooming.
bernese mountain dog: The Bernese Mountain Dog is a large working breed from the canton of Bern in the Swiss Alps and one of the four Sennehund, or Swiss Mountain breeds. Known for their distinctive tricolor coat of black, white, and rust, these fluffy, gentle giants weigh between 70 and 115 pounds. They are known for their calm and affectionate temperament, earning them a reputation as devoted family companions. Bernese Mountain Dogs are also intelligent and relatively easy to train, making them well-suited for active families with children. They were first bred to pull carts, herd livestock, and protect their families from predators. Although they were first brought to the United States in the 1920s for farm work, today, this devoted breed serves as a working dog, therapy dog, service animal, and companion.
bichon frise: The Bichon Frise (pronounced BEE-shawn FREE-say) is a small-sized companion breed originally from the Mediterranean region. While less common, this dog breed is also referred to as Bichon à Poil Frisé or BichónTenerife. Known for their affectionate nature, Bichons make wonderful pets for those looking for a small dog that doesn’t shed — or at least doesn’t shed much. Even with hypoallergenic dogs, it’s hard to determine how hypoallergenic they’ll be.
biewer terrier: The Biewer Terrier, also called the Biewer Yorkie, is a rare purebred dog created through the occurrence of a recessive piebald gene in two Yorkshire Terriers. These dogs are friendly, playful, and energetic, which are some of their best qualities. The Biewer Terrier, pronounced like “beaver,” is also known by the names Biewer à la Pom Pon, or Biewer Yorkshire. You may be able to find Biewer Terrier dogs at shelter and rescues, so remember to adopt! It’s always better than shopping!
black and tan coonhound: The Black and Tan Coonhound, one of six Coonhound breeds, is an outgoing, friendly, and athletic dog. While they can be highly skilledworking dogs, they also make great companions. These beloved dogs bring joy to homes and serve as playmates, jogging or walking partners, and even lovable companions for days spent lounging on the sofa.
black mouth cur: The Black Mouth Cur is a medium—to large-sized working breed that was first bred to help farmers and hunters in the rugged terrain of the southeastern United States. This breed excels as a hunter and usually catches and kills game on the ground, although they will also tree and bay game. Beyond their hunting abilities, these working dogs can also herd and protect livestock, guard the home, and track game. Of course, they also make wonderful companion dogs with the right owners.
black russian terrier: The Black Russian Terrier, often referred to as the BRT or the “Black Pearl of Russia,” is a large and powerful breed known for its imposing presence and protective nature. This breed was originally developed in the mid-20th century in the Soviet Union for military and police work. It is a crossbreed, with influences from various breeds such as the Rottweiler, Giant Schnauzer, Airedale Terrier, and Newfoundland. The Black Russian Terrier is characterized by its solid black coat, which is dense, coarse, and slightly wavy. They have a distinctive beard and mustache, which adds to their striking appearance.
bloodhound: The Bloodhound is a large scent hound breed whose origins date back to medieval France where they were Initially bred for trailing deer, boar, and rabbits. Since their early origins, they have evolved into a highly active and intelligent breed. Their exceptional sense of smell earns them a vital role in law enforcement and search and rescue operations. Fans of this breed adore their gentle disposition and iconic appearance.
blue lacy: The Blue Lacy, or Lacy Dog, is full of energy, smart, and easy to train. They do best when they have a job to do and plenty of room to move around. Despite their name, their coats can be red or tri-colored, but they all have the blue color gene. Originating in 19th-century Texas, Blue Lacys were bred for herding, hunting, and ranch work. Their robust nature, adaptability, and quick learning abilities make them unsuitable for apartment living or novice owners due to their high intensity and energy.
bluetick coonhound: The fast and muscular Bluetick Coonhound dog breed stands out for their striking coat. They have a pleasantly pleading expression and a big bawl mouth — which means they have a long, drawn-out bark. Although they are primarilyhunters, Blueticks can be fine housedogs and love their people.
bocker: The Bocker is aCocker SpanielandBeaglehybrid who embodies the small size, affection, and curiosity of their parent breeds. Although labeled a designer breed, these pups, also known as Beakers or Beagle Spaniels, can still be found in shelters and breed-specific rescues.
boerboel: At first glance, you might think the large, intimidating Boerboel would make an excellent guard dog, and you’d be correct. However, this breed is equally known for being loving, calm, and family-friendly, especially towards human children. South African farmers first bred these protectors to defend their homesteads from hyenas, lions, and all manner of deadly wildlife. As an added bonus, these guardians also offered invaluable companionship. The Boerboel, pronounced “boo-r-bull,” gets its name from Dutch/Afrikaans words that roughly translate to “farmer’s dog.” This gigantic homestead companion also garnered the a variety of names, including the South African Mastiff, South African Boerboel, Borbull, or Bole.
boglen terrier: The Boglen Terrier, also called the Boston Beagle Terrier or Boggle, is a cross between theBeagleandBoston Terrierdog breeds. These dogs are known to be playful, spunky, loyal,and at times, stubborn. This lovable and perky mix has proven to become quite popular among owners from various backgrounds.This mixed breed can sometimes be mistaken with a Boxer and Beagle mix, which also goes by the name Boggle.
bolognese: A true companion dog, the Bolognesedog breedloves to be at their family’s side. However, they also love getting their way and can be quite crafty about it, so be careful — you could find yourself being manipulated by a ten-pound furball.
borador: The Borador is an intelligent and energetic hybridbreed, combining theherding prowessof theBorder Colliewith the even-tempered nature of theLabrador Retriever. This cross produces a lively, devoted family dog equally made for outdoor adventures or snuggling on the couch. With an eagerness to please and a highly trainable disposition, the Borador makes a wonderful addition to an active home.
border collie: The Border Collie is a medium-sized breed originally developed to herd sheep in the hilly border region between Scotland and England. Known for their remarkable speed, agility, and intense “eye”—a focused stare used to control their flock—Border Collies are also renowned for their boundless energy, stamina, and strong work ethic, making them top-tier herding dogs. Typically, they are a healthy breed with a lifespan of 12 to 15 years, although they can be prone to certain health issues such as hip dysplasia and collie eye anomaly.
border sheepdog: The Border Sheepdog is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theBorder CollieandShetland Sheepdogbreeds. Energetic, intelligent, and playful, these pups inherited some of the best traits from both of their parents.
border terrier: The Border Terrier is a small-sized rough-coated terrier breed from the English-Scottish border originallybredto assist in foxhunts by driving foxes out of their hiding places and out into the open for the hounds to chase. Nowadays, they make great family members who adore their humans.
bordoodle: The Bordoodle, also called the Borderdoodle, is a medium-to-large-sized crossbreed between theBorder Collieand thePoodle. This uniquePoodle mixhas a well-muscled frame with a coat that ranges from gently waved to curly. With their alert expression and an air of quiet confidence, the Bordoodle is a winning combination of two beloved breeds.
borzoi: The Borzoi, also known as the Russian Wolfhound, is a member of thehoundgroup. This ancient and noble canine exudes strength and agility with its long, slender body and distinctly shaped head. With its large, expressive eyes and keen awareness, the Borzoi Hound is always on alert, ready for action. Borzois are not the most playful dogs, but they do enjoy a good walk or run. They require moderate grooming and are relatively easy to train, which can be great for any new pet parent welcoming a Borzoi puppy to their home.
boshih: The BoShih is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theShih TzuandBoston Terrierbreeds. Small, inquisitive and affectionate, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
bossie: The Bossie is a relatively newdog breedwho combines the lively personality of theBoston Terrierwith the herding instincts and intelligence of theAustralian Shepherd. These pups have a robust, compact build from their Boston Terrier parent along with the floppy ears, short muzzle, and expressive eyes that give them an adorably scrunched face. However, their coats take after the Australian Shepherd side with a medium-length double layer in a variety of colors and patterns like black, red, blue merle, or red merle. Bossies make engaging companions with their friendly, clownish antics tempered by the Aussie’s natural eagerness to please their owners.
boston boxer: The Boston Boxer is a mixed breed dog–a cross between theBoston TerrierandBoxerdog breeds. Friendly, intelligent, and playful, these pups inherited some of the best traits from both of their parents.
boston terrier: The Boston Terrier is a compact, small companion breed that originated in the United States and is famous for their distinctive “tuxedo” markings, which are white with black, brindle, or seal (dark brown) coloring. Small in size, Boston Terriers stand 12 to 17 inches tall at the shoulders and weigh less than 25 pounds, depending on their weight class. The Boston Terrier temperament is friendly, affectionate, intelligent, and can be strong-willed. They are also highly adaptable and are suitable for apartment living.
bouvier des flandres: The Bouvier des Flandres is a large working breed from Belgium who was originallybredto be a versatile farm dog. With a muscular build, the Bouvier de Flandres weighs 70 to 100 pounds and stands 23 to 26 inches at the shoulder. This athletic breed helped farmers with many tasks, includingherding livestock(particularly cattle), pulling carts, and guarding. While the hard-working and intelligent Bouvier is still an ideal farm dog,they also make great service animals, law enforcement, and guard dogs.
boxador: The Boxador is a mixed breed of dog–a cross between theBoxerandLabrador Retrieverdog breeds. Medium to large in size, intelligent, and totally devoted to their families, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
boxer: The Boxer is a medium-to-large-sized Mastiff-type breed that originated in Germany in the 19th century. Historians believe that this breed descended from the now-extinct Bullenbeisser, which hunters used for hunting large game like boar and deer. They developed the modern Boxer by crossing the Bullenbeisser with various other breeds, including theEnglish Bulldogand theMastiff.
boxerdoodle: The Boxerdoodle, typically a medium-sized dog breed, is a mix between theBoxerandPoodle. Friendly, smart, and playful, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents. Boxerdoodles are also sometimes known asBoxerpoos. Like other Poodle mixes, you can stil find these designer “doodle” dogs in shelters and breed specific rescues, so remember to always adopt! Don’t shop if you’re looking to add one of these dogs to your home!
boxmatian: The Boxmatian is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theBoxerandDalmatiandog breeds. Playful, goofy, and protective, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
boxweiler: The Boxweiler is a large crossbreed between theBoxerandRottweiler. Loving, loyal, and bright, these pups inherited some of their parents’ best qualities. Boxweilers go by several names, including Box Rotty, Boxer Rottie, and Boxie Rottie.
boykin spaniel: Boykin Spaniels are friendly, confident, and adaptable dogs that make great companions for families and individuals. They are affectionate and loyal, and they love being around people. They are also patient andgentle with children, and they typically get along well with other dogs. While prized for their highly skilledhunting skills, these friendly dogs are loved for their charm, personality, and adaptability.
bracco italiano: The Bracco Italiano is a large-sized Sporting dog from Italy and one of the oldest pointing breeds. Tough and reliable, the Bracco Italiano has been a beloved huntingdog breedin their native Italy for thousands of years but only joined the American Kennel Club (AKC) in 2022. In recent years, the Italian Pointer gained notice as afamily companionwith a sweet and affectionate personality. Their long ears, droopy lips, soulful expression, and lean build give the gentle Bracco Italiano a distinctive and rugged look. Believed to be an ancient breed, this Italian favorite dates back to the fourth or fifth century B.C. The Bracco Italiano goes by several other names, including the Italian Pointer, The Italian Pointing Dog, and Bracco.
braque du bourbonnais: The Braque du Bourbonnais is a medium-sized, ancient purebred pointing dog originally from France. Affectionate, calm, and intelligent, these pups have some of the best qualities of any dog breed out there today. This breed goes by several other names, including Bourbonnais Pointer, Bourbonnais Pointing Dog, French Pointing Dog, French Pointer, Braques Francaises, and Braque Bourbonnais.
briard: The Briard, also known as the Berger de Brie, is a medium-to-large-sized herding breed originally from France. The Briard is perhaps most easily recognized by their shaggy coat which ranges in color from solid black, fawn, or gray to various shades of tawny. Beyond their unique appearance, the breed is renowned for their intelligence and loyalty. They excel in various roles from herding livestock to serving as a faithful family companion and guardian. Their gentle yet protective nature makes them excellent with children and a natural watchdog. With their agility and trainability, Briards have also found success in dog sports and obedience competitions.
brittany: The Brittany, formerly known as Brittany Spaniel, is a compact yet energeticsporting dog breed, Originating from the Brittany region of northwestern France, this well-muscled gundog is a favorite of hunters for its endurance and versatility in the field.
broholmer: The Broholmer, also called the Danish Mastiff, is a large working dog from Denmark originally used for stag hunting. Despite their imposing appearance as a Mastiff-type breed, the Broholmer’s temperament is known to be calm, protective, and friendly. Powerfully built with a strong, muscled body and a large head, the Broholmer stands 27 to 30 inches tall at the shoulder and can easily weigh up to 150 pounds. Although first bred as a hunter, the Broholmer later became guardians of homes and farms. While relatively rare outside of Denmark, the Broholmer has increased in popularity in the United States.
brussels griffon: The Brussels Griffon is a small and charming dog breed that originated in Belgium. They were originally bred asrat catchersin stables, but their adorable looks and delightful personality quickly endeared them to people as companion dogs. You can easily recognize a Brussels Griffon for their adorable, furry gargoyle appearance, which includes a short, pushed-in face with large, expressive eyes. They have a wiry and dense coat that can come in different colors, such as red, black, black and tan, or belge (a mix of black and reddish-brown). Their ears are may be cropped to stand upright, enhancing their alert and attentive expression.
bugg: The Bugg, a cross between theBoston TerrierandPug, is an intelligent, loving, and spunky companion dog. The Bugg’s combination of unique Pug and Boston Terrier traits results in a dog with a comical, clownish appearance and an entertaining, affectionate personality. They are loyal and devoted to their families, and are also known for their playful, energetic, and sometimes mischievous natures.
bull-pei: The Bull-Pei is a mixed breed dog–a cross between theChinese Shar-PeiandEnglish Bulldogbreeds. Medium in size, loving, and loyal, these pups inherited some of the best traits from both of their parents.
bull terrier: The English Bull Terrier, also called the Bull Terrier, is a medium-sized terrier breed from the United Kingdom known for their unique “egg-shaped” head and glossy coat. Perhaps one of the most iconic-looking dog breeds, they can immediately be recognized for their muscular build and sloped foreheads, a hallmark of their appearance. To the outside eye, these Pit Bull dogs also can have a somewhat imposing appearance, but these pups are just as goofy and comical as can be.
bullador: The Bullador is a mixed breed dog–a cross between theLabradorandEnglish Bulldogbreeds. These pups fall into the medium-to-large size range. Active and loyal, Bulladors inherited some of the best traits from both of their parents.
bullboxer pit: The Bullboxer Pit is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theBoxerandAmerican Pit Bull Terrierdog breeds. Powerful, faithful, and energetic, these pups inherited some of the best traits from both of their parents.
bulldog: The English Bulldog, also called the Bulldog or British Bulldog, is a medium-sized Mastiff-type dog breed in the non-sporting group known for their distinctive appearance and friendly disposition. Dating back to the 13th century, this breed has evolved from their bull-baiting past to become gentle and affectionate companions. They can easily be recognized for their unique features, including a wrinkled face and pushed-in nose, muscular build, and compact body. As a breed standard, Bulldogs stand 12 to 15 inches tall at the shoulder and weigh up to 50 pounds. Females are slightly smaller, usually only weighing up to 40 pounds.
bullmastiff: The Bullmastiff is a large, muscular dog bred in England in the late 1800s as a guard dog to protect gamekeepers and their property from poachers. Bullmastiffs have a gentle and loyal nature, however, they can also be very protective of their family and home.
bullmatian: The Bullmatian is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theBulldogandDalmatiandog breeds. Loving, energetic, and friendly, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
cairn terrier: The Cairn Terrier is a small working terrier developed on the Isle of Skye in Scotland. If you’re a fan of The Wizard of Oz, you already know the breed. Farmers used them to rid their property of vermin, and they needed a dog with courage, tenacity, and intelligence — characteristics still found in today’s Cairn.
canaan dog: The Canaan Dog, a pariahbreed, boasts an ancient lineage that has thrived in Israel’s desert for millennia. Many believe it’s the same breed that Hebrews used in biblical times to herd and safeguard their flocks and settlements, a role it continues to fulfill amongBedouinsandDrusecommunities today.
cane corso: The Cane Corso, also known as the Italian Mastiff, is a large working dog  originally bred as guardians of farms and livestock. These powerful dogs possess a loyal and protective nature. However, their imposing stature and past association with dog fighting have unfortunately led to misconceptions about their temperament.
cardigan welsh corgi: The Cardigan Welsh Corgi, the older of the two Corgi breeds, is a herding dog believed to have existed in Wales for over 3,000 years. Throughout history, farmers relied on these speedy, hardworking, and versatile dogs to drive cattle to market. These days, they mostly love to spend time with their families.
catahoula bulldog: The Catahoula Bulldog is a fierce and affectionate hybrid breed resulting from the crossbreeding of theCatahoula Leopard Dogand theAmerican Bulldog. Known for their striking appearance and versatile nature, they are a capable companion for various roles. With their muscular build, intelligent demeanor, and protective instincts, the Catahoula Bulldog has gained popularity among dog owners seeking a loyal and hardworking canine companion.
catahoula leopard dog: The Catahoula Leopard Dog, or Catahoula Cur, is a medium to large herding dog from Louisiana originally bred to hunt hogs. Because they were bred to work in swamps and forests, they are tough dogs that require a firm and consistent leader with time to keep them occupied. Interestingly, Catahoulas are named after the Catahoula Parish in Louisiana, where they were first bred.
caucasian shepherd dog: The Caucasian Shepherd Dog or Caucasian Ovcharka Dog is a fierce family protector originating in the Caucasus Mountain region, specifically Georgia, Azerbaijan, Armenia, and Dagestan. They’re also known as Russian Bear Dogs, Baskhan (Karachay) Pariy, or just CO, and many variants exist. This massive breed is highly territorial and won’t back down from a fight, even against bears or wolves. Caucasian Shepherd Dog temperament is intelligent, alert, protective, and calm, but their independent nature can make them difficult to train, and they are not recommended for novice owners. Their natural distrust of strangers and other animals can also lead to aggressive tendencies if an experienced trainer doesn’t keep them in check.
cav-a-jack: The Cav-a-Jack is a mixed breed dog–a cross between theCavalier King Charles SpanielandJack Russell Terrierdog breeds. Energetic, friendly, and playful, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
cavachon: The Cavachon is a small-sized companion crossbreed between aCavalier King Charles Spanielwith aBichon Frise. Combining the Cavalier’s affectionate nature with the Bichon’s playful demeanor, the Cavachons are beloved for their sweet temperament, intelligence, and irresistible appearance. Sporting a soft, fluffy coat in various colors, including white, apricot, and cream, these affectionate dogs have expressive eyes and floppy ears.
cavador: The Cavador is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theCavalier King Charles SpanielandLabrador Retrieverdog breeds. Friendly, loving, and intelligent, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
cavalier king charles spaniel: The Cavalier King Charles Spaniel is a small-sized companion breed from the United Kingdom, renowned for their affectionate and loving personality. Standing 12 to 13 inches tall at the shoulder, these affectionate dogs are classified as toy members of the Spaniel group. Named after the British monarch who popularized the breed, they thrive on human interaction and make excellent companions for individuals and families. Their warm and gentle temperament also makes them well-suited for households with children and other pets. They are known for their friendly, patient, and loving personalities. They form strong attachments with their human companions and do not do well when left alone for long periods of time.
cavapoo: The Cavapoo, also known as the Cavoodle, is a small-to-medium-sized hybrid breed, a cross between aCavalier King Charles Spanieland aPoodle. These dogs are also known by several names, including Cavadoodle and Cavapoodle. Outgoing, playful, and curious, Cavapoos inherit some of the best traits from their parent breeds. Like many other designer dog breeds, the exact origins and development timeline of the Cavapoo are not well-documented. However, they have quickly become popular in the designer dog world, particularly within the “Doodle dog” category.
cesky terrier: The Cesky Terrier, also called the Bohemian Terrier, is a Czech terrier breed that descends from theScottish TerrierandSealyham Terrier. Originally developed in the mid-20th century to hunt vermin, the Cesky Terrier’s loving personality, moderate exercise needs, and small size make them a great companion dog.
chabrador: The Chabrador is a mixed breed dog–a cross between theChow ChowandLabrador Retrieverdog breeds. Loyal, friendly, and independent, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
cheagle: The Cheagle is a delightfully quirky cross between theChihuahuaandBeagledog breeds. These spunky little dogs range from 10-15 inches tall and tip the scales at 15-25 pounds, keeping them well in the small-sized range. With their compact size and energetic dispositions, Cheagles make ideal city-dwelling companions for active owners. Cheagles go by several names, including Beagle Chi, Chi-Bea, Beagle Chihuahua mix, and Chibeagle.
chesapeake bay retriever: The Chesapeake Bay Retriever is a versatile and hardworking breed known for its exceptionalhuntingand retrieving abilities. With a muscular and athletic build, this breed is perfectly suited for sporting. Their keen sense of smell and sharp instincts make them outstanding trackers, capable of locating game even in challenging environments.
chi chi: The Chi-Chi is a hybrid companiondogthat blends the unique look of theChinese Crestedwith the sassy spirit of theChihuahua. These diminutive yet spirited canines can inherit either the hairless or coated variety from their Crested parent, boasting a sleek, low-shed coat and a well-proportioned frame.
chi-poo: The Chipoo or Chi-Poo is an adorable and cuddly crossbreed resulting from the mix of aChihuahuaand typically aMiniature Poodle. Weighing in typically between 5 to 15 pounds and standing at around 6 to 10 inches tall, Chi-Poos boast a compact yet sturdy build, ideal for households seeking a small-sized canine companion. Their coat varies in texture and color, often inheriting the hypoallergenic qualities of the Poodle, making them suitable for allergy sufferers.
chigi: The Chigi is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theChihuahuaandCorgidog breeds. While they may be small, they’re very alert, affectionate, and playful. These pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
chihuahua: The Chihuahua, or the Chihuahueño in Spanish, is a toy-sized companion breed from Mexico that comes in short and long-haired varieties. While pint-sized, these little dogs are powerhouses with big personalities. Weighing in at just 3 to 6 pounds, Chihuahuas are known for their feisty attitude, loyalty, and love for attention. They make excellent companions for people of all ages and lifestyles. Chihuahuas can build deep connections with all family members, but they often have a penchant for singling out one household member as their “person.” This doesn’t mean they don’t care about others. Rather, they have a unique way of showing undivided loyalty and affection to the one they feel closest to.This breed loves nothing more than being with their people and requires minimum grooming and exercise. They also make excellent apartment dogs.
chilier: The Chilier, a newer crossbreed, emerges from the mixing of two beloved breeds. This breed is a hybrid of theCavalier King Charles Spanieland theChihuahua. Despite their small stature, Chiliers possess a significant personality, making them a lovely companion adaptable to various lifestyles.
chinese crested: Chinese Crested dogs are a small, hairless breed known for their affectionate and playful personalities, as well as theirdistinctive appearance.  Chinese Cresteds are the perfect couch potato companions. They can read your mind and will lie in bed for hours without moving a muscle. Despite their laziness, Chinese Cresteds are athletic dogs. They can jump surprisingly tall fences and compete in agility.
chinese shar-pei: The Chinese Shar-Pei is a medium to large-sized working breed originally used for guarding, hunting, and herding. Unfortunately, the breed’s inherent strength and protective nature were exploited and the breed was later used for dogfighting. They nearly went extinct in the 20th century but made a comeback as popularity rose. Fortunately, in modern times, they’ve earned a much-deserved place as a devoted family companion.
chinook: The Chinook  (pronounced Sha-Nook) is a rare sled dog breed that originated in the White Mountains of New Hampshire. In the early 1900s, Arthur Treadwell Walden purposely bred this athletic and hardy dog to assist in hauling freight across the harsh New England winters.
chion: The Chion is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theChihuahuaandPapillondog breeds. Petite, playful, and loyal, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents
chipin: The Chipin is a small-sized crossbreed between the spunkyChihuahuawith the determinedMiniature Pinscher. These dogs typically inherit the best traits from both parent breeds, combining the Min Pin’s spirited and confident nature with the Chihuahua’s affectionate and loyal demeanor. Chipins are small in size, usually weighing between 5 to 15 pounds, with a height of 8 to 12 inches. Their appearance can vary, but they often have short, sleek coats and come in a variety of colors, including black, brown, tan, and cream. Due to their small size and bold personalities, Chipins make excellent companions for apartment dwellers and families looking for a lively, compact dog.
chiweenie: The Chiweenie is a small cross between theChihuahuaandDachshunddog breeds. Known for their playful, affectionate, and loyal temperament, these pups inherited some of the best traits from both of their parents. Chiweenies are also known by several names, including Choxie, Weeniehuahua, the German Taco, and the Mexican Hot Dog.
chorkie: The Chorkie is a loyal and adorable small dog hybrid created by mixing the spiritedChihuahuawith the lovingYorkshire Terrier. These tinydogstypically stand just 5-10 inches tall at the shoulder and only weigh between 4-10 pounds once fully grown. With their small size, moderate exercise needs, and minimal shedding, Chorkies make ideal apartment or condo dogs. Their coats can be a range of colors like black, tan, blue, or steel gray.
chow chow: The Chow Chow is an ancient medium-to-large-sized working dog breed that originated in China. Known for their lion-like appearance and distinctive blue-black tongue, this unique dog has a rich history that dates back thousands of years. They originally served various purposes, including hunting, guarding, and pulling carts. Today, people primarily know them as loyal companions and family pets.  These regal dogs have a unique appearance characterized by their sturdy build, a lion-like mane, and a thick double coat that comes in various colors, including red, black, blue, cinnamon, and cream.
chow shepherd: The Chow Shepherd is a mixed breed dog–a cross between theChow ChowandGerman Shepherddog breeds. Medium to large in size, protective, and playful, these pups inherited some of the best traits from both of their parents.
chug: The Chug, a delightful and affectionate mixeddog breed, is a cross between the Chihuahua and the Pug. This small-sized dog is often characterized by its charming appearance, featuring a compact body, a short snout, and expressive eyes. Chugs typically have a short, smooth coat that can come in various colors, and their overall appearance often combines the characteristics of both parent breeds. They are typically outgoing and love being the center of attention, making them excellent companions for families and individuals alike.
chusky: The Chusky is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theChow ChowandSiberian Huskydog breeds. Curious, headstrong, and loving, these dogs inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
cirneco dell’etna: The Cirneco dell’Etna (Pronounced “cheer-NECK-oh del et-na”) is a purebred dog from the island of Sicily. Gentle, alert, and independent, these pups have some of the best traits of anydog breedaround today. Cirneco dell’Etnas goby many other names, including Cirneco, Sicilian Hound, Sicilian Greyhound, Sicilian Rabbit Hound, and Sicilian Rabbit Dog. These dogs can make great pets and show dogs, and you may find them at local shelters or rescues. Remember, it’s always better to adopt and not shop!
clumber spaniel: The Clumber Spaniel, a medium to large-sized sporting breed, is the largest member of the Spaniel family and originates from Nottinghamshire, England. The breed originated within the Clumber Park estate, where the Duke of Newcastle selectively bred them in the 18th century for their exceptional hunting abilities. With their robust build and powerful scenting capabilities, the Clumber Spaniel served primarily as a proficient bird dog, adept at flushing and retrieving game from dense undergrowth. While still valued for their hunting prowess, Clumbers have also gained recognition as a beloved family companion, cherished for their affectionate nature and laid-back temperament.
cockalier: Friendly, gentle, and intelligent, the Cockalier is a rising star in the dog world.  A hybrid breed, these pups inherited some of the best traits from both of their parents. You may find these small dogs in shelters and breed-specific rescues, so remember to always prioritize adoption.
cockapoo: The Cockapoo is a cross between thePoodleandCocker Spanielbreeds. A small dog with the personality of a big clown, the Cockapoo dog is perhaps the first of the “designer dogs” (not a purebred dog breed), dating back to the 1960s rather than just a few years ago. Thanks to their curly or wavy coats and big eyes, they often look like teddy bears. Cockapoos have the friendly temperament of the Cocker Spaniel and the hypoallergenic coat of the Poodle. Depending on the Poodle parent, they come in various sizes and exhibit multiple coat colors and textures. While brown and cream are the most common, merle, blue, and black Cockapoos are rarer.
cocker spaniel: The Cocker Spaniel, or the American Cocker Spaniel, is a small-sized Spaniel breed and the smallest member of the sporting group. Standing 13 to 15 inches tall at the shoulder and weighing between 20 to 30 pounds, the Cocker Spaniel is compact and athletic. This breed is active, affectionate, energetic, and always eager to participate in family activities. They thrive on human companionship and are known for forming strong bonds with their owners.
collie: The Collie, or the Lassie dog, is a medium-to-large-sized dog breed known for its long, flowing coat and gentle temperament. There are two varieties; Smooth Collies and Rough Collies. The smooth has a shorter, flatter coat, while the rough has a long, thick coat that requires regular grooming.
corgi inu: The Corgi Inu is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theShiba Inuand theCorgidog breeds. With the adorable looks of a fox, these vigilant, affable pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
corgidor: The Corgidor is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theCorgiandLabrador Retrieverdog breeds. Mid-sized, even tempered, and energetic, these pups inherited some of the best traits from both of their parents.
corman shepherd: The Corman Shepherd is a playful and intelligentdog breedthat comes from the crossbreeding of aCorgiand aGerman Shepherd. This unique blend results in a medium-sized dog with the signature short legs of the Corgi and the noble stature of the German Shepherd. Known for their intelligence and versatility, Corman Shepherds inherit the herding instincts from their Corgi lineage and the protective nature from their German Shepherd ancestry. Their coat may vary in length and color, often showing a mix of the distinct markings and patterns found in both parent breeds.
coton de tulear: The Coton de Tuléar is a small dog breed named after the city of Tuléar in Madagascar. This smart little dog is believed to be related to theBichon Friseand theMaltese. Standing 9 to 11 inches high and weighing only 8 to 13 pounds, Cotons make the perfect compact companion. With a sweet and clownish personality, these fluffy white pups get along well with everybody. That includes kids and other dogs! Highly adaptable, they can easily fit in with apartment dwellers or live in larger homes. They are easily trainable, low-maintenance, and even thrive with novice or first-time pet parents.
curly-coated retriever: Created to retrieve game from land or water, the Curly-Coated Retrieverdog breedwas popular with English gamekeepers, hunters, and poachers alike. Today they compete in such dog sports as field trials, agility, obedience, and flyball. They’ve even work as therapy dogs, drug sniffing dogs, and search and rescue dogs. When they’re not working or competing, they’re happy to snuggle up beside their favorite person and get a nice back scratch.
dachsador: The Dachsador is a mixed breed dog–a cross between theDachshundandLabrador Retrieverdog breeds. Lively, active, and friendly, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
dachshund: The Dachshund, affectionately called the Wiener Dog, Sausage Dog, and Doxie, is a small German scenthound with short legs and an elongated body first bred to hunt tunneling animals like rabbits or foxes. Hunters even used packs of Dachshunds to trail wild boar, which might be surprising considering the reputation for how dangerous wild boar can be. But certainly, this shows that the Dachshund has a sense of courage to outmatch their size. Any Doxie owner knows this to be true! As legendary literary critic and humorous journalist H. L. Mencken said, “Half a dog high and a dog and a half long,” but Dachshunds are enough to take on a badger. That’s how they got their name (Dachsmeaningbadger;Hundmeaningdog).
dalmatian: The Dalmatian is a medium-sized spotted dog breed that originated in the Dalmatian region of Croatia.Probably most famous today as firehouse mascots, this breed has a long history of assisting firefighters when they would run ahead of the fire carriages and clear the way of other dogs who nipped at the horses. As dignified dogs, the aristocracy in England quickly adopted Dalmatians after they were brought to the country in the 17th century.
dandie dinmont terrier: Dandie Dinmont Terriers originally werebredto hunt otter and badger. Nicknamed the gentleman of theterrier family, they’re calm and reserved, yet retain their terrier tenacity and love of the hunt. Independent yet easy-going and affectionate, these dogs provide plenty of love to their humans. Additionally, they do not demand a great deal in way of exercise or grooming needs. Still, they don’t appreciate being left alone for long hours of the day.
daniff: The Daniff is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theEnglish MastiffandGreat Danedog breeds. Lovable, intelligent, and protective, these pups inherited the best qualities from both of their parents.
deutscher wachtelhund: The Deutscher Wachtelhund, commonly known as the German Spaniel, stands out as a versatile and accomplishedhunting dogoriginating from Germany. Bred for their proficiency intracking, retrieving, and pointing, this medium-sized breed showcases an athletic build and a distinctive wavy coat that comes in various colors. Originating in the late 19th century, the German Spaniel has earned a reputation as a superb all-around hunting companion, excelling in various terrains, including water and dense cover.
doberdor: The Doberdor is a mixed breed dog–a cross between theDoberman PinscherandLabrador Retrieverdog breeds. Intelligent, protective, and loyal, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
doberman pinscher: The Doberman Pinscher is a large working dog breed first bred in Germany in the late 19th century by Karl Friedrich Louis Dobermann, a tax collector whose job often put him in harm’s way. To mitigate the risks associated with his profession, Dobermann set out to create a dog breed to protect him as he traveled through dangerous areas. The result was the Dobermann Pinscher, a muscular dog breed with a temperament characterized by loyalty, fearlessness, and devotion.
docker: The Docker is a crossbreed dog that results from combining theDachshundandCocker Spanielbreeds. Their size ranges from small to medium and they find joy in both lounging and playful activities such as fetching, hiking, or chasing squirrels. These pups have inherited the finest qualities from their parents. Dockers are known by various names, including Doxie Spaniel and Spaniel Doxie. You might encounter these mixed-breed dogs in shelters and rescues despite being classified as designer breeds.
dogo argentino: The Dogo Argentino, sometimes called the Argentinian Mastiff or the Argentine Dogo, is a strong, athletic, and loyal breed. They can be both fierce hunters and gentle protectors of their humans. These active dogs also have a high prey drive, a strong will, and, at times, a distrust of strangers and other animals. They may not be suitable for homes with cats unless they’ve been properly socialized. They will need an experienced dog owner to handle the breed. While this breed sometimes has a poor reputation, they can be incredible companions with the proper training and socialization.
dogue de bordeaux: The Dogue de Bordeaux, also known as the Bordeaux Mastiff or French Mastiff, is a large and imposingdog breedwith a rich history. This is a massive and muscular dog with a stocky build. They have a distinctive head with a prominent forehead, deep wrinkles, and a powerful jaw. Dogue de Bordeaux’s eyes are typically dark and set wide apart. They have medium-sized ears and hang down. They have a short and dense coat – which comes in various shades of fawn, often with a mask on the face.
dorgi: The Dorgi, sometimes spelled the Dorgie, is a small-to-medium-sized cross between theDachshundandCorgidog breeds. These pups are known for their loyalty, intelligence, and friendly nature, having inherited some of the best traits from both of their parent breeds. With their bright personalities and sharp minds, Dorgis are highly trainable. They are affectionate and devoted and tend to form strong bonds with their human companions. They are especially good with children thanks to their gentle and affectionate temperament.
dorkie: The Dorkie is an adorable and playful blend of theDachshundandYorkshire Terrier. These unique pups stand out with their appearance and personality. Combining the distinctive characteristics of their parent breeds, the Dorkie typically exhibits a small yet sturdy build, with a long body like the Dachshund and a silky, often multi-colored coat reminiscent of the Yorkshire Terrier. These playful and affectionate dogs tend to form strong bonds with their families, displaying loyalty and devotion to their owners. While they may inherit the spirited and lively temperament of both parent breeds, Dorkies are known for their intelligence and adaptability, making them suitable companions for various living situations, including apartments or family homes.
doxiepoo: If you were the type of kid who wasn’t afraid to choose the “mystery” flavor of candy in a bag, a DachshundPoodle Mix— or Doxiepoo — may be for you. While this mixed breed can have famously endless combinations of traits, they are known for being affectionate, intelligent, and playful. The list of names the Doxiepoo is known by is as long as the parentDachshund: Dachdoodle, Doxiedoodle, Doxiepoodle, Dachshunddoodle, and Dachshundpoo. This is one of the most unpredictable pairings among mixed breeds, even within a single litter. This means they often end up in shelters. However, with such winning traits, they are wonderfully adoptable dogs. And, as one of the most affectionate mixed breeds, they’ll fall in love with you before you leave the shelter.
doxle: The Doxle is a cross between the spiritedDachshundand the softheartedBeagledog breeds. Affectionate, playful, and inquisitive, the Doxle combines some of the best characteristics of both parents. Doxles are also known as Beaschunds, Beweenies, and Doxies. Despite their “designer breed” status, you can find Doxles at breed-specific rescues and shelters. So please opt to adopt!
drentsche patrijshond: The Drentsche Patrijshond is a purebred dog hailing from the Netherlands. Intelligent, loyal, and sensitive, these pups have some of the best qualities of any dog breed. Drentsche Patrijshonds go by several other names, including Dutch Partridge Dog and Drent. Renowned for their gentle temperament and adaptability, this breed has been a cherished companion and skilledhunting partnerfor centuries. Originating from Dutch hunting traditions in the 16th century, the Drentsche Patrijshond has deep roots in the Netherlands, where it played a vital role in assisting hunters in tracking and retrieving game. Beyond their hunting prowess, the Drentsche Patrijshond is prized for their affectionate nature and strong bonds with its human family.
drever: The Drever, a unique and charming Swedishdog breed, is known for its short stature and strong, compact build. Originally developed forhunting purposes, this dog breed excels at tracking and driving game, such as deer and boar. Drevers are characterized by their long, low body, short legs, and a distinctive, expressive face with dark eyes and floppy ears. Their dense, weather-resistant double coat comes in a variety of colors, including red, black, and tan, and requires minimal grooming.
dutch shepherd: Originally used by shepherds in the Netherlands for all-around farm work, the Dutch Shepherd is an intelligent, highly trainable jack-of-all-trades. In modern times, they find jobs as search and rescue dogs, service animals, and family-friendly companions.
english cocker spaniel: The English Cocker Spaniel is a medium-sized gundog from England whose name derives from their proficiency in hunting woodcock. With expressive eyes and a wagging tail, this breed has won the hearts of dog lovers worldwide. Their silky, medium-length coat is one of the breed’s standout features and is often found in various colors and patterns including black, blue roan, orange roan, black and tan, tricolor, and more. English Cocker Spaniels stand 15 to 17 inches at the shoulder and weigh up to 35 pounds by the breed standard. Of course, there is always variation due to genetics.
english foxhound: The English Foxhound was originally a huntingdog breed, designed to pursue foxes in the English countryside. Today they also enjoy life as a family companion and compete in the conformation ring. Many English Foxhounds also compete in Foxhound Performance Trials, which grade the dogs on their pack hunting skills. As hunting dogs, English Foxhounds have high energy and need plenty of exercise during the day.Apartment dwellersbeware, as this pup needs room to run and may “speak up” quite a bit, which may not foster the best relationships with nearby neighbors. Meet the breed’s exercise needs, and you’ll have a playful and affectionate member of the family.
english setter: The English Setter is a captivating and versatiledog breed, known for its charming personality and elegant appearance. Originally developed as ahunting dogin England, English Setters have a rich history dating back hundreds of years. Their journey began as skilled hunting companions, honing their natural instincts to locate and point game with precision. With their keen sense of smell and intelligence, English Setters quickly earned recognition as “the moderate setter.”  The English Setters lively spirit and affectionate nature make them outstanding companions for active families and individuals alike.
english springer spaniel: The English Springer Spaniel is a medium-sized gun dog in the sporting group traditionally used for flushing and retrieving game. Friendly, intelligent, and cheerful, these athletic Spaniels were primarily bred for their versatility and keen scenting abilities. Over time, their devoted and adaptable personalities caused them to be admired for more than their sporting abilities, and soon, they found their way into homes as companions worldwide. The English Springer Spaniel’s temperament is affectionate, energetic, playful, intelligent, and alert. While the breed continues to thrive alongside sporting hobbyists and competitors alike, they also excel in various working roles, including search and rescue, due to their incredible sense of smell. They are also highly sensitive and empathetic, making them great candidates as service animals, especiallypsychiatric service dogs.
english toy spaniel: The English Toy Spaniel is a small, gentle, and affectionate dog breed. Nicknamed “Charlies” after the king who loved them, the English Toy Spanieldog breedis quieter and more reclusive than their Cavalier cousins. While similar to this close relative, the two are distinctly different breed. They are known for their long, silky coats, large, dark eyes, and long, floppy ears. English Toy Spaniels are typically between 10 and 12 inches tall and weigh 8 to 14 pounds. They are moderately active dogs that require daily walks and playtime. They don’t need nearly as much exercise as more athletic breeds — a casual stroll each day will do — and they mostly prefer to stay inside.
entlebucher mountain dog: The Entlebucher Mountain Dog, also called the Entlebucher Sennehund or simply the Entle, is a loyal and agile Swiss breed. Originally bred as cattle drovers and farm dogs in the Entlebuch region of Switzerland, they are highly intelligent and excel in obedience and herding tasks. Their alert and protective nature makes them excellent watchdogs, and their strong work ethic ensures they thrive with active families who provide ample exercise and mental stimulation. While affectionate with their human companions,Entlebucher Mountain Dog puppiesrequire early socialization and consistent training to channel their energy and natural instincts effectively.
estrela mountain dog: The Estrela Mountain Dog is a large livestock guardian breed originating from the rugged terrain of the Serra da Estrela mountains in Portugal. They are also known as the Cão da Serra da Estrela. One of the oldest dog breeds in Portugal, these powerful dogs have been loyal companions and fierce livestock protectors in their homeland. With their muscular build and thick, weather-resistant coat, Estrela Mountain Dogs easily adapt to the challenging environment of the mountains. While formidable and independent, the Estrella Mountain Dog temperament is also intelligent, devoted, and dignified. To that end, these dogs are natural protectors, always vigilant and ready to defend their territory from predators.
eurasier: The Eurasier is a intelligent and versatile companion, known for its striking wolf-like appearance and affectionate nature. Originating in Germany, the Eurasier was developed in the 1960s by crossing theChow Chow,Wolfspitz, andSamoyedbreeds. This deliberate breeding aimed to create a dog that combined the best qualities of its parent breeds to become the ultimate family dog. The Eurasier’s beautiful, thick double coat comes in a variety of colors.
field spaniel: The Field Spaniel, a medium-sized dog breed of the spaniel type, is known for its gentle and sensitive nature. Until the 1800s, British spaniels utilized for flushing gamebirds lacked the breed classification seen today. Instead, they were grouped based on size and role. This dynamic shifted in the late 1800s with the rise of dog shows in England. The need for distinct types and breeds emerged, leading to the establishment of the Field Spaniel. This breed was born from crosses ofCocker,English Springer, andSussex spaniels. They quickly gained popularity in the show ring while proving their prowess as skilled hunters.
finnish lapphund: The Finnish Lapphund is an ancient, medium-sized herding breed from Finland in the Spitz family. They are one of the oldest dog breeds in the world, with evidence of their existence dating back to 7000 BC. Along with adding an aesthetic flair to this cold-weather-loving dog, the Finnish Lapphund’s dense fur provides insulation that allows this unique breed to excel in Finland’s harsh, cold climates. Their coats come in various colors, including black, white, sable, brown, and red. Additional physical features of the Finnish Lapphund include triangular, upright ears and oval-shaped eyes. Often, this breed has distinct mask-like markings referred to as “spectacles” around their eyes. Beyond their eye-catching appearance, the Finnish Lapphund excels at herding, a skill developed over thousands of years as a working dog to the indigenous people of present-day Scandinavia and Russia.
finnish spitz: The Finnish Spitz is a small-sized hunting breed known for their striking flame-colored, fox-like appearance. This rare dog has been a beloved companion and hunter in Finland for centuries, where they hold the esteemed title of Finland’s national dog. Their alert and energetic temperament makes them an excellent choice for active families or individuals. Though they may initially be mistaken for aShiba Inu, the Finnish Spitz dog is an entirely different breed.
flat-coated retriever: The Flat-Coated Retriever is a medium—to large-sized gun dog breed from England who was originally bred as a dual-purpose retriever of game on land and water. They also compete in obedience, rally, and agility and are ideal companions for people who are looking for an active dog breed. However, apartment-dwellers should beware, as these pups need room to run and play. They can adapt to city living, but prospective owners should keep up with daily walks and play sessions to meet this breed’s high exercise needs.
fox terrier: The Wire Fox Terrier is a medium-sized terrier breed originally used for fox hunting in England. Fox Terriers are canine classics who’ve changed little since their emergence in the late 18th century. There are two types: Smooth Fox Terriers are sleek, elegant, and intelligent, while Wire Fox Terriers are handsome, happy, and quite stylish when groomed properly. With their wiry and dense double coat, triangular head, dark eyes, and small V-shaped ears, this terrier exudes an alert and intelligent expression. Coupled with a well-muscled build and a spirited but cocky demeanor, Wire Fox Terriers are not only visually striking but also highly energetic and playful.
french bulldog: The French Bulldog, or the Frenchie, is a small companion breed from France that resulted from the crossbreeding of imported English Toy Bulldogs with Parisian ratting dogs. With their unique charm and dynamic personality, the French Bulldog has become a beloved breed among dog lovers everywhere. Over the years, these lovable pups have captured the hearts of many, catapulting them into the most popular dog breed worldwide.
french spaniel: The French Spaniel is a purebred dog from France and Canada. These dogs are sociable, intelligent, and gentle, making up some of any pooch’s best qualities. The French Spaniel goes by several names, including Epagneul Francais, French Setter, and Canadian Setter. Despite their status as a pure breed, you may find these dogs in shelters and rescues, so remember to adopt!
frenchton: The Frenchton is a small-to-medium-sized cross between theBoston TerrierandFrench Bulldog, two breeds known for their compact size and sociable demeanor. Sturdy, playful, and easygoing, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents. Frenchtons also go by the names Frenchbo, Faux Frenchbo, and Froston. Despite their status as a designer breed, you can find these pups, in shelters and breed-specific rescues. Always prioritize adoption if you’re considering bringing a Frenchton puppy home.
frengle: The Frengle is a mixed dog breed–a cross between theFrench BulldogandBeagledog breeds. Charismatic, affectionate, and even-tempered, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
german pinscher: The German Pinscher, or the Standard Pinscher, is a medium-sized breed known for its sleek, elegant appearance, intelligence, and spirited personality. Originally from Germany, these hardworking dogs performed various roles, including hunting vermin and guarding homes and farms. They have a distinct, well-proportioned build with a short, glossy coat.
german shepherd dog: The German Shepherd Dog, known as the Alsatian if you’re living in the United Kingdom, is a large working breed from Germany prized for their incredible intelligence and unwavering loyalty. The German Shepherd is highly intelligent and trainable, and their unique abilities are often put to use.
