Because of their inherent desire to work, German Shepherds often find roles in law enforcement, including bomb detection and forensics. They also excel in search and rescue, therapy work, and as service dogs. While we tend to think of German Shepherds serving in these modern-day roles, as their name indicates, they were first used for herding.
german shepherd pit bull: The German Shepherd Pit Bull mix, often referred to as a “Shepherd Pit” or “Shep-Pit,” is a unique and dynamic crossbreed that combines the characteristics of two distinct and powerful dog breeds. TheGerman Shepherdis renowned for its intelligence, loyalty, and versatility. They often stand out as police, search and rescue, and service dogs. On the other hand, theAmerican Pit Bull Terrieris known for its strength, agility, and affectionate nature. When these two breeds are combined, the result is a hybrid that inherits a blend of both physical and temperamental traits.
german sheprador: The German Sheprador is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theGerman ShepherdandLabrador Retrieverbreeds. Large, energetic, and loyal, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
german shorthaired pointer: The German Shorthaired Pointer, often referred to as the GSP dog, is a versatile and energetic breed known for their athleticism and intelligence. Originating in Germany, the GSP dog was developed in the 19th century with the intention of creating a multipurposehunting dogcapable of working on both land and water. This beloved breed is medium to large-sized with an athletic build. They have a sleek coat that comes in various colors and patterns. The coat is low maintenance and provides protection against different weather conditions. The German Shorthaired Pointer temperament is characterized by a friendly and outgoing nature, loyalty, affection, and an energetic but gentle disposition.
german spitz: The German Spitz is a small to medium-sized companion breed and one of the oldest dog breeds originating from Europe. Attentive, energetic, and devoted, these pups have some of the best qualities of any dog breed. German Spitzes go by several other names, such as Spitz, German Spitz Mittelspitz, and Deutscher Spitz. If you’d like to bring home a German Spitz puppy, research the requirements for care and training.
german wirehaired pointer: The German Wirehaired Pointer, also referred to as the GWP or the Deutsch Drahthaar, is a versatile and energetic sporting dog breed. This beloved breed has a distinctive wire-haired coat and a well-muscled build. Known for their strength, endurance, and adaptability, GWP’s often perform well in varioushuntingand outdoor activities.
giant schnauzer: The Giant Schnauzer was created to be a working dog breed, so they have intelligence and drive. They can be a loyal and courageous companion for the person who can provide them with the training, exercise, and attention they need. These pups originated in Germany, where they had jobs like driving cattle, working in butcher shops and stockyards, and even guarding breweries. Although this is a pure breed of dog, you may find them in shelters and rescues, so remember to adopt! Don’t shop if you decide this is the dog for you.
glen of imaal terrier: The Glen of Imaal Terrier is a strong, independentdog breednamed for the remote valley in Ireland where they originated. Bred to hunt fox and badger, and to keep homes free of rodents, the Glen is a skilled and cunning hunter. They’re also an affectionate family dog, gentle, and loving with their people. Even though these are purebred dogs, some may still end up in the care of shelters or rescues. Consider adoption if this is the breed for you.
goberian: The Goberian is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theGolden RetrieverandSiberian Huskydog breeds. Friendly, energetic, and smart, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
goldador: The Goldador, also known as the Golden Labrador, is a hybrid dog breed that results from crossing aGolden Retrieverwith aLabrador Retriever.These dogs truly embody the best of two of the most popular dog breeds. They inherit the best traits from both beloved parent breeds, making them excellent family pets and service animals. Goldadors typically exhibit both Retrievers’ friendly and outgoing nature, and they easily get along with children and other pets. Regarding temperament, Goldadors are known for their intelligence, trainability, and eagerness to please, which makes them highly responsive to obedience training.
golden cocker retriever: The Golden Cocker Retriever is a mixed breed dog–a cross between theGolden RetrieverandCocker Spanieldog breeds. Sweet, playful, and intelligent, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
golden mountain dog: The Golden Mountain Dog is a large to-giant-sized crossbreed between aGolden Retrieverand aBernese Mountain Dog. Gentle, friendly, and intelligent, these pups have inherited some of the best qualities from their parent breeds. Standing between 24 and 28 inches tall at the shoulder and weighing between 65 and 140 pounds, the Golden Mountain Dog can vary greatly in size due to the distinctive differences in their parent breeds. They also feature a dense, water-resistant coat in shades of gold and black.
golden retriever: The Golden Retriever is a medium-sized retrieving breed from Scotland known for their gentle nature and silky golden coat. The Golden’s affectionate and playful temperament makes them great family pets for individuals and households. They are also highly intelligent and empathic and often excel in roles asservice dogsor therapy dogs. It’s no surprise that the Golden Retriever is among the top ten most popular dogs in the United States!
golden retriever corgi: The Golden Retriever Corgi is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theCorgiandGolden Retrieverdog breeds. Loyal, silly, and active, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
golden shepherd: The Golden Shepherd is a medium-to-large-sized cross between theGolden RetrieverandGerman Shepherddog breeds. Typically, this breed stands 22 to 26 inches tall at the shoulder and weighs 60 to 100 pounds. Regarding temperament, the Golden Shepherd combines both parent breeds’ intelligence, loyalty, and versatility. As a result, these dogs make excellent companions for families and individuals alike.
goldendoodle: The Goldendoodle is a popular hybrid of aPoodleand aGolden Retrieverand is one of the most beloved designer dog breeds. While not a breed recognized by purebred organizations, these crossbreeds combine the best qualities of their parent breeds. Known for their friendly and sociable nature, Goldendoodles are excellent family pets, service animals, and therapy dogs. Their intelligence and eager-to-please demeanor make training Goldendoodle puppies relatively easy.
gollie: The Gollie is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theGolden RetrieverandColliedog breeds. Loving, clever, and energetic, these pups inherited some of their parents’ best qualities. Gollies are also sometimes known asGolden Collies. You can find these mixed-breed dogs in shelters and breed-specific rescues, so remember to always adopt! Don’t shop if you want to add one of these wonderful dogs to your home!
gordon setter: The Gordon Setter is a large sporting dog breed from Scotland originally bred to hunt pheasants and quail. Today, they are still fine hunting companions and field trial competitors, although many have made their way into homes as pets worldwide. These “black and tans” have an alert, confident, and curious temperament and are loyal and affectionate with their people. However, they can be aloof with strangers. Early socialization is essential.
great dane: The Great Dane is an extra-large working dog breed that originated in Germany. Known for their impressive size and friendly demeanor, they are often referred to as a “gentle giant.” Standing as one of the tallest dog breeds, Great Danes have a commanding presence with a strong, muscular build and a sleek, well-proportioned body. Despite their imposing stature, they are known for their gentle and affectionate nature, making them good family dogs.
great pyrenees: The Pyrenean Mountain Dog, also known as the Great Pyrenees, is a large-to-giant-sized working dog originally bred to protect flocks and herds in the Pyrenees Mountains of France and Spain. Also called Le Chien de Montagne des Pyrenees in their native French, this gentle guardian stands up to 32 inches tall at the shoulder and can weigh up to 150 pounds. While fiercely protective of their flock, they make wonderful companions and are particularly fond of children, making them good house dogs. The Great Pyrenees temperament is known for being laid-back, easygoing, and independent.
greater swiss mountain dog: The Greater Swiss Mountain Dog, often referred to simply as the Swissy, is a majestic and robust breed known for its strength, loyalty, and gentle nature. Originating in the Swiss Alps, this breed has a rich history of serving as a working dog, primarily used for herding cattle and guarding farms. It is one of the four Swiss Mountain Dog breeds, along with theBernese Mountain Dog, theAppenzeller Sennehund, and theEntlebucher. Among them, the Swissy is the largest.
greyador: The Greyador is a mixed breed dog–a cross between theLabradorandGreyhounddog breeds. These pups fall into the medium-to-large size range. Gentle yet strong, Greyadors inherited some of the best traits from both of their parents.
greyhound: The Greyhound is the fastestdog breedin the world, with a sleek, aerodynamic body and long, powerful legs that allow it to reach speeds of up to 45 mph. They were originally bred to hunt small game, and their speed and agility made them excellent at catching rabbits, foxes, and other prey.  Not surprisingly, Greyhounds made a name for themselves as racing dogs. Greyhounds are the second fastest animals on Earth, after the cheetah.
hamiltonstovare: The Hamiltonstovare is a medium-sizedhound breedfrom Sweden that was originally developed as a versatile hunting dog. Known for their striking tricolor coat, the Hamiltonstovare is muscular and possesses a keen sense of smell, making them exceptional scent hounds. Their breed was developed for fox hunting and is recognized for their endurance, agility, and determination in tracking and treeing game. Beyond their hunting prowess, the Hamiltonstovare are characterized by their friendly and social nature. Their well-balanced temperament, intelligence, and trainability further contribute to their adaptability as both skilled working dogs and loyal household members.
hanoverian scenthound: The Hanoverian Scenthound is adog breedoriginating from Germany. They are loyal, independent, and calm pups who have some of the best qualities of most dog breeds around today. The Hanoverian Scenthoundgoes by several other names, including Hanoverian Hound, Hanoverian Scent Dog, and Hannoverscher Schweisshund. Despite their status as a rare dog breed, you can still try to find these beautiful pups in shelters or breed specific rescues, so remember to adopt! Don’t shop!
harrier: TheHarrier dog breedis a dynamic medium-sized scenthound known for their sleek physique and enthusiastic personality. With a history dating back several centuries, the Harrier is believed to have originated in medieval England, where they were developed to hunt hare and fox in packs. With a sturdy build, expressive eyes, and colored coat, the Harrier has a striking and handsome presence. Renowned for their keen sense of smell and exceptional stamina, the breed excels in scent-tracking activities.
havanese: The Havanese is a small toy dog breed from Cuba with a rounded face, floppy ears, and either curly or straight long hair. They have friendly, affectionate, and playful personalities. These dogs are very intelligent, easy to train, and have alow prey drive.
hokkaido: The Hokkaido, an intelligent and alertpurebred dogfrom Japan, is a devoted companion. Hokkaido dogs are also known by the names Ainu-ken, Seta, and Ainu dog. In Japan, their name is often shortened to Do-ken. Though rare outside of Japan, Hokkaido dogs can still be found in shelters and rescues.
horgi: The Husky Corgi mix, often referred to as a “Horgi,” is a delightful crossbreed that combines the distinct characteristics of the a cross between theSiberian HuskyandPembroke Welsh Corgidog breeds. This hybrid typically inherits the striking appearance of the Husky, featuring a dense double coat. Their appearance is not defined and their coat may come various color patterns. The Corgi influence is evident in their sturdy build and short legs. As a result, this charming mixed breed brings together the intelligence and independence of the Husky with the affectionate and loyal nature of the Corgi.
huskita: Akita Huskies, fondly called the Huskita, is a truly special crossbreed. These energetic dogs merge theSiberian Husky‘s playfulness with theAkita‘s noble demeanor, creating a truly dynamic dog breed. Additionally, the Husky’s endurance complements the Akita’s strength and loyalty. Because both breeds originated for different purposes, one for working and one for guarding, this dog breed is a formidable yetaffectionatecompanion. Each Huskita possesses a unique personality that reflects the influence of both parent breeds.
huskydoodle: The Huskydoodle, also called the Huskypoo, is a medium-sized crossbreed between theSiberian Huskyand the Poodle. This hybrid breed goes by several other names, including the Husky Poodle, Siberpoo, Poosky, and Siberian Poodle. This beloved breed inherits the Husky’s distinctive coat markings and the Poodle’s hypoallergenic qualities. To that end, these dogs can be appealing for individuals with allergen sensitivities. Known for their social and outgoing demeanor, the Husky Poodle mix thrives on companionship and makes an excellent family pet. Their intelligence and agility make them well-suited for various activities, including obedience training and agility courses. However, potential owners should be prepared for their energetic nature and commit to providing regular exercise and mental stimulation.
ibizan hound: The Ibizan Hound is a medium-sized sighthound that was originally bred to hunt rabbits and small game on the Balearic island of Ibiza. Ibizan Hounds are still used for this purpose in Spain and other countries. They also compete in lure coursing, agility, obedience, conformation, and tracking. In addition, they are beloved family companions.
icelandic sheepdog: The Icelandic Sheepdog, also known as the Icelandic Spitz or Íslenskur fjárhundur in Icelandic, is a breed of dog originating from Iceland. They are one of the oldest and only nativedog breedsof Iceland. The Icelandic Sheepdog, believed to be companions to the ancient Vikings, actively protected flocks from birds of prey.
irish red and white setter: The Irish Red and White Setter is a beloved medium-to-large breed known for its elegant aesthetic and calm companionship, making it a popular choice among families and sporting enthusiasts. Breed enthusiasts cherish thesebird dogsfor their beauty, friendly nature, and strong work ethic. They adapt well to living with children and other pets, although their natural prey drive requires some caution around small animals.
irish setter: The Irish Setter is a large-sized gundog from Ireland, first bred as a versatile hunting dog. Boasting an elegant and athletic build, the Irish Setter can immediately be recognized by their striking, silky mahogany or chestnut-colored coat. With an affectionate and outgoing temperament, Irish Setters are often described as “redheads with a heart of gold.” They also form strong bonds with their families and are known for their playfulness and enthusiasm.
irish terrier: The Irish Terrier is a medium-sized terrier breed from Ireland known for their lively and intelligent personalities. One of the oldest terrier breeds, they were once described as the “poor man’s sentinel, the farmer’s friend, and the gentleman’s favorite.” Rugged and stouthearted, these Irish dogs have the advantages of a convenient size and versatile abilities as a companion and watchdog.
irish water spaniel: Originating in Ireland, the Irish Water Spaniel features a dense, curly, water-resistant coat that sets it apart from other spaniels. With a sharp and playful personality, Irish Water Spaniels are highly intelligent and excel in various tasks, including hunting waterfowl and retrieving objects from the water. These active dogs are known for their natural swimming abilities and webbed feet, making them well-suited for water-related activities. They are loyal and affectionate companions, often forming strong bonds with their families, and their active and lively nature makes them well-suited for families and individuals who enjoy outdoor adventures and activities.
irish wolfhound: The Irish Wolfhound is a giant sighthound breed, known for their towering size and strength, which has fueled myths and legends throughout history. Once believed to have been used in battle to pull men from horses and chariots, these powerful dogs wereoriginally bred to hunt large gamelike deer, boar, and wolves. In Ireland, the ownership of these remarkable dogs was restricted by law to kings and nobles, with the number of dogs one could possess directly linked to the status and prestige associated with their title. Today, the Irish Wolfhound is a versatile companion
italian greyhound: The Italian Greyhound was favored by noblewomen in Italy during the Middle Ages. But beyond being aristocratic companions, these small sighthounds had the speed, endurance, and determination to hunt small game. Today, they are family dogs admired for their beauty and athleticism.
jack-a-poo: The Jack-A-Poo is mixed breed dog–a cross between theJack Russell TerrierandPoodledog breeds. Generally the Poodle parent is Miniature or Toy sized, as opposed to Standard. Affectionate, lively, and intelligent, Jack-A-Poos are energetic pups who make great family dogs.
jack chi: The Jack Chi, also known as the Jackahuahua, is a small cross between theJack Russell TerrierandChihuahuadog breeds. Friendly, playful, and energetic, these spunky companions inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents. Weighing roughly 8 to 18 pounds, the Jack Chi is compact and sturdy, similar to their Jack Russell Terrier lineage, with a short, sleek coat that comes in various colors including white, tan, black, and combinations.
jack russell terrier: The Jack Russell Terrier, considered distinct from the Russell Terrier and theParson Russell Terrier, is a lively and independent terrier breed developed around 200 years ago to hunt foxes in South England. While they are charming and affectionate, they’re also a handful to train and manage and may be best suited for experienced dog parents.
japanese chin: The Japanese Chindog breedhails from Asia, where they’ve been prized as acompanionfor more than a thousand years. They were a popular member of Chinese and Japanese imperial courts, and it was in Japan that their distinctive look was developed.
japanese spitz: The Japanese Spitz is a small to medium-sized companion breed in the Spitz family, known for their elegant white coats and foxlike appearance. This courageous dog breed may look similar toAmerican Eskimo Dogs, whitePomeranians, or smallSamoyeds, but Japanese Spitzes have their own lineages. Although not native to Japan, like theShiba InuorAkita, these white fluffy dogs were developedin the island nation. Japanese Spitz dogs are also intelligent, easy to train, low-maintenance, and great with children. They make good apartment dogs, so long as pet parents meet their exercise needs. While their gorgeous, white fur may seem difficult to maintain, these charming pups have fairly low grooming needs.
korean jindo dog: The Korean Jindo Dog is a fiercely loyal companion withunmatched devotionto their owners. Indeed originating in South Korea, these intelligent dogs have a natural knack for hunting, tricks, and agility. Although, the Jindo Dog is fastidious and quiet indoors, making them great household pets. In fact, these special dogs are illegal to export from Korea without a permit.
karelian bear dog: The Karelian Bear Dog, also known as Karjalankarhukoira, hails from Finland. It is a robust and vigilant breed renowned for its fearlessness. They possess the remarkable ability tohuntand even tree various game, including bears, lynxes, wild boars, wolves, and moose. In Finland, the Karelian Bear Dog holds a special place as one of the ten most popular dog breeds. To integrate them successfully into a household, extensivesocializationand training are essential. Additionally, this breed has high energy levels and requires ample exercise andmental stimulation.
keeshond: The Keeshond is an olddog breed, once a companion and watchdog on the barges and boats that traveled the canals and rivers of Holland in the 17th and 18th centuries. He’s almost exclusively acompanion dogtoday.
kerry blue terrier: The Kerry Blue Terrier is the quintessentialworking dog. They hail from County Kerry, Ireland, where they were bred to hunt small game and birds, kill rodents, andherdsheep and cattle. Intelligent and brave, they became a cherished pet as well, displaying fierce devotion to their family or pack.
komondor: The Komondor, also known as the Hungarian Sheepdog, is a large working breed from Hungary that was primarily used as a livestock guardian breed. Primarily recognized for their unique corded coat, giving them the informal name of the “mop dog.” This mop-like appearance served a valuable purpose and would help them blend in with herds of sheep and allow them to jump out and surprise predators. A powerful breed, Komondors stand 25 to 27 inches tall at the shoulder and weigh up to 100 pounds. They possess strong protective instincts, a trait once valued primarily for working purposes but now beloved as part of this unique breed’s temperament.
kooikerhondje: The Kooikerhondje is a small spaniel-type huntingbreedfrom the Netherlands. Suspected to be an ancestor of theNova Scotia Duck Tolling Retriever, this lively breed hails from Holland and was used to lure ducks into traps for hunting or tagging. They still serve as hunting dogs but excel at other dog sports like flyball. They’ve also made their way into homes as beloved companion animals. The Nederlandse Kooikerhondje personality is lively, self-confident, intelligent, and easygoing. They are highly trainable and are very compatible with children.
kuvasz: The Kuvasz is a large, white,flock-guarding dogfrom Hungary. They are protective of their people and suspicious of strangers. These dogs think for themselves and can be challenging to train. In Hungarian, the plural of Kuvasz is Kuvaszok.
kyi-leo: The Kyi-Leo is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theMalteseandLhasa Apsodog breeds. Playful, lively, and sweet, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
lab pointer: The Lab Pointer is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theLabrador RetrieverandPointerdog breeds. Energetic, loyal, and intelligent, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
labernese: The Labernese is a large, lovable, and loyalcrossbreedthat combines the best traits of theBernese Mountain Dogand theLabrador Retriever. This hybrid dog has a thick coat, often tri-colored, and a muscular yet graceful build. Their gentle, affectionate nature contradicts their impressive size, making them loyal and devoted companions.
labmaraner: The Labmaraner is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theLabrador RetrieverandWeimaranerdog breeds. Intelligent, active, and friendly, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
labrabull: The Labrabull is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theAmerican Pit Bull Terrierand theLabrador Retrieverdog breeds. Medium in size, energetic, and loyal, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
labradane: The Labradane is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theLabrador RetrieverandGreat Danedog breeds. Loyal, affectionate, and playful, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
labradoodle: The Labradoodle is a medium-to-large-sized hybrid cross between a Labrador Retriever and a Poodle. Known for their intelligence, friendliness, and social temperament, Labradoodles are highly adaptable to various living environments and make great family pets. Labradoodles often inherit the hypoallergenic, low-shedding coat of the Poodle, which appeals to individuals with allergies. Their coats can range from wavy to curly, and they come in various colors, including cream, chocolate, black, and apricot.
labrador retriever: The Labrador Retriever, often known simply as the Labrador or Lab, is a British breed of gundog and one of the most popular dog breeds worldwide. With their iconic floppy ears, expressive eyes, and wagging tails, Labs exude charm and warmth. Renowned for their athletic abilities and eagerness to please, Labs excel in various roles, from therapy work to search and rescue. The Labrador’s gentle and patient temperament also makes them exceptional companions for children, earning them the reputation of being one of the best family dogs.
labrastaff: The Labrastaff is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theLabrador RetrieverandStaffordshire Bull Terrierdog breeds. Athletic, loyal, and intelligent, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
labsky: The Labsky, also known as the Siberian Retriever or Huskador, is a medium to large-sized cross between theLabrador RetrieverandSiberian Huskydog breeds. Active, smart, and loyal, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents. Despite their high-end image, you may find these mixed-breed dogs in shelters and rescues.
lagotto romagnolo: The Lagotto Romagnolo, or Italian Water Dog, was originally bred to hunt waterfowl through the wet marshlands of Romagna in Italy, but today it is best known for its truffle hunting skills. Consequently, with many of the marshlands drained, the Lagotto has found a new purpose. Humans can easily train these dogs to use their keen sense of smell for scent work. Their thick coats help them stay warm in the fall and winter, and protect them from thorns and debris as they run through forests.
lakeland terrier: The Lakeland Terrier was firstbredto hunt the foxes that preyed on sheep during the lambing season in Northern England’s Lake District. Today’s Lakies, as these lively, feisty little dogs are nicknamed, are affectionate, friendly, and self-confident. Even though these are purebred dogs, some may still end up in the care of shelters or rescues. Always prioritize adoption if this is the breed for you.
lancashire heeler: The Lancashire Heeler is a small to medium-sized working dog who was once used to drive livestock to market and hunt rats and rabbits. They are a beloved companiondog breedin their native Britain, although they are considered anendangered breed by the Kennel Club. Lancashire Heelers resemble theirCorgicousins and have an intelligent, mischievous, and sometimes stubborn temperament. That can makethem difficult to train, so they’ll probably do best with an experienced pet-parent.
leonberger: The Leonberger is a large, gentle giant of a dog that originated in Germany. The jumbo-sizeddog breedis a mix of theNewfoundland, longhairedSaint Bernard, andGreat Pyrenees. They are known for their lion-like appearance, with a long, thick coat that can be golden, red, or brown. Leonbergers are also known for their loyalty, intelligence, and trainability. They make great family dogs and are known to be good with children. However, they do require a lot of exercise and grooming.
lhasa apso: The Lhasa Apso is a small companion breed originally bred to be a watchdog in the monasteries of Tibet. While the Lhasa is no longer a revered sentinel, the breed is still cherished as a loyal family companion worldwide. Don’t be fooled by their small size — the Lhasa Apso is also full of spunk!
lhasapoo: Protective, loyal, and playful, the Lhasapoo is a relatively new Poodle mix. Thesehypoallergenic dogsinherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents. Although they are considered a designer breed, always prioritize adoption when looking for your future forever friend.
lowchen: The Lowchen, often referred to as the “Little Lion Dog,” is a small and lively toy breed that boasts a distinctive lion-like mane and a friendly, affectionate nature. With a compact and well-balanced body, Lowchen typically stand between 10 to 13 inches tall at the shoulder and weigh around 9 to 18 pounds. Their luxurious, long, and wavy coat can come in various colors, including shades of black, white, lemon, and chocolate. Regular grooming is necessary to maintain the coat’s health and prevent matting.
maltese: The Maltese is a small, white, hypoallergenic companion breed from the Mediterranean island of Malta. They are playful and affectionate and have been prized for centuries. Although they’re full of energy, the Maltese requires only occasional exercise to keep them healthy and happy. A short walk or playtime in the yard usually satisfies their needs. They are not high-maintenance dogs, and their long hair must only be brushed a few times a week.
maltese shih tzu: The Maltese Shih Tzu is a small companion hybrid dog breed of theMalteseandShih Tzu. Created with the same goal asDoodles— to be a small, allergy-friendly companion who doesn’t shed much — this hybrid is a sweet lapdog who likes to play with the kids. They are also known as the Mal-Shi, Malti zu, and the Malt-Tzu.
maltipoo: The Maltipoo is a cross between aMalteseand aPoodle, another hybrid breed in the recently popular category of doodle dogs. Alert and active, the Maltese Poodle mix is known for their adorable appearance, friendly disposition, and hypoallergenic coat. These small dogs come in various colors, from creamy white and apricot to chocolate and silver. With their intelligent and trainable nature, the Maltese Poodle thrives in active homes where they can be the center of attention.
manchester terrier: The lively, spirited Manchester Terrierdog breedis atrue terrier. Bred in Manchester, England for ratting and rabbit coursing, this dog has game and they love to show it. The Gentleman’s Terrier (as they’re known in Victorian England) is not a sparring dog but loves a good chase. This makes them a flyball and agility rock star.
mastador: The Mastador is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theMastiffandLabrador Retrieverdog breeds. Large, energetic, and lovable, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
mastiff: The English Mastiff, perhaps the most well-knownMastiff breedaside from theTibetan Mastiff, is one of the largest dog breeds, known for their massive size, muscular build, and gentle temperament. Originating from ancient Molossian war dogs in Greece, their exact lineage is debated, but they are believed to have played a significant role in the breed’s development. Historically, the breed was employed by the Roman Empire in warfare, showcasing their bravery and strength. As time progressed, these dogs found a new purpose in medieval Europe, where they became valued guardians of castles, estates, and livestock.
miniature pinscher: The Miniature Pinscherdog breedwas originally bred in Germany to hunt vermin, primarily rats, in homes and stables. Many people think that they were developed by breedingDobermansto progressively smaller sizes, and indeed, they look similar to Dobermans. However, the Min Pin is a distinct and much older breed. This breed is good for people who live in apartments or small homes. They do not require a lot of exercise, but they do enjoy going for walks and playing fetch. They are also good for families with children, as they are patient and gentle.
miniature schnauzer: The Miniature Schnauzer is a small, intelligent, and active dog breed from Germany easily identified by their “Schnauzer cut.” They are known for their wiry, double coat that comes in black, white, or salt and pepper. Miniature Schnauzers are playful and loyal dogs that make great companions for families. They require moderate exercise and are relatively easy to train, which comes in handy if you become a pet parent to a Miniature Schnauzer puppy.
morkie: The Morkie, also called the Morkshire Terrier, is a cross between theMalteseand theYorkshire Terrierknown for their affectionate personalities. As one might expect, this little dog inherits the best traits from both parent breeds. With a small stature and a beautiful, silky coat that often combines the colors and patterns of the Maltese and Yorkshire Terrier, the Morkie is an adorable addition to any family. Their playful and energetic nature makes them well-suited for indoor and outdoor activities, and they thrive on interaction with their human family members. Due to their small size, Morkies are often adaptable to apartment living, provided they receive the necessary daily exercise and mental stimulation.
mudi: The Mudi, pronounced “moody,” is a highly trainableherding dogrenowned for its alertness, activity, and intelligence. They excel in various tasks such as watchdog duties, hunting, agility, obedience competitions, and search and rescue missions. Undoubtedly, this lesser-known dog is a rare gem of the Hungarian herding group.
mutt: A mutt, also known as a mixed-breed dog, is a dog who does not belong to a specific, recognized breed but instead has unintentional ancestry from two or more breeds. These dogs come in various sizes, shapes, colors, and temperaments. Mutt genetics often result in unique and unpredictable combinations of traits, making each one distinct. Due to their mixed heritage, Mutt dogs are less prone to many hereditary diseases that purebred dogs might inherit. They are highly adaptable and can be found in various roles, from beloved family companions to service and working dogs.
neapolitan mastiff: The Neapolitan Mastiffdog breedwas developed in southern Italy as a family and guard dog. Today this massive breed is known as a gentle giant. Some fans of the breed affectionately call them the Neo, and others know them as Mastino. Although these are purebred dogs, you may still find them in shelters and rescues.
newfoundland: The Newfoundland, lovingly called the “Newfie,” is a large and gentle working breed from Canada first developed for working with fishermen. Historically, Newfoundlands also worked as draft animals, pulling carts and sleds, as well as assisting fishermen by hauling nets or carrying supplies. You might spot one of these gorgeous dogs by their fluffy coat and impressive size. Beyond their size and iconic appearance, Newfoundland dogs are renowned for their exceptionalwater rescue abilities. One of the defining characteristics of Newfoundlands is theirwebbed feet, making them excellent swimmers. Their large lung capacity and muscular build allow them to stay afloat and assist in saving lives.
norfolk terrier: The Norfolk Terrier is a small working terrier breed known for their lively and spirited personality. Originating in the United Kingdom, these dogs are small in size but big in heart. They have a distinctive wiry coat that comes in various shades of red, wheaten, black and tan, or grizzle. Norfolk Terriers are characterized by their alert expression, floppy ears, and docked tails. Despite their small stature, they possess a feisty and fearless nature that makes them excellent watchdogs. They are nearly identical to theNorwich Terrier, the ears being the only difference between the two.
norwegian buhund: The Norwegian Buhund belongs to the spitz type of dog breeds and shares close relations with theIcelandic Sheepdog. Known for their versatility, these dogs often serve as watchful guardians. Originating from the western coastlands of Norway, the Norwegian Buhund was first bred by the Vikings. The breed’s name is derived from the Norwegian words “bu,” meaning farm or livestock, and “hund,” meaning dog. Buhunds were tasked with protecting farms andherding cattle and sheep. In addition to their notable working abilities, they also became cherished family companions.
norwegian elkhound: With astriking wolf-like appearance, the Norwegian Elkhound is amedium to large-size dog breedknown for theirbold spirit and courage. Originally bred for hunting, tracking, and herding, these multipurpose dogs were an asset to the people of Scandinavia for hundreds of years. Despite their name including “hound,” these dogs are actually part of the Spitz group. Athletic and intelligent, Elkhounds would independently track prey and hold it at bay while signaling to hunters with their piercing barks and howls. Such a job would require incredible stamina and endurance.
norwegian lundehund: The Norwegian Lundehund, often referred to as the “Puffin Dog,” is a unique and rare breed known for its distinctive features and history. Originating from Norway, this small spitz-type dog was originally bred for the purpose of hunting puffins.The Lundehund has several remarkable traits, including its six toes on each foot. This unique feature provides exceptional grip and agility on rocky terrain. Their flexibility is also noteworthy, making them incredibly agile and well-suited for navigating narrow and treacherous cliffs.
norwich terrier: The Norwich Terrier originated as a ratter on farms but moved up in the world to bolt foxes from their dens during hunts. Today, they’re amusing companions who serve double duty by keeping your home and yard free of rats and other vermin. Norwich Terriers are small and sturdy with a wiry coat. This delightful breed exhibits independence, playfulness, and a jovial personality. As one might expect, these cherished dogs makeexcellent family companions.
nova scotia duck tolling retriever: The Nova Scotia Duck Tolling Retriever, also known as the “Toller,” is a medium-sizedgundog. This cheerful canine companion was originally bred for hunting purposes. Because of its smaller size stature, people often mistake this red-coat breed for a miniature version of theGolden Retriever, one of its larger retriever relatives. Developed for the purpose of luring and retrieving waterfowl, Tollers demonstrate remarkable versatility in various domains.
old english sheepdog: The Old English Sheepdog, known to fans as the English Sheepdog, is a large, athletic dog breed with an unmistakable shaggy coat. Historically, thisdroverhelped farmers drive cattle and sheep to market. Today, this good-natured dog enjoys the comfort of home life and still competes in conformation, obedience, agility, and herding trials.
otterhound: The Otterhound is a large scenthound breed from England known for exceptional tracking abilities and incredibly rare status as an endangered breed. With only a few hundred registered Otterhounds worldwide, this extremely rare breed was historically developed to assist hunters in pursuing and capturing otters, hence their name. Otterhounds are characterized by their shaggy and waterproof double coat, which protects challenging terrains and water. Their expressive eyes and distinctive nose give them an endearing look. Otterhounds are renowned for their strong sense of smell, a crucial trait for their historical role in tracking otters along riverbanks.
papillon: The Papillon, a spirited and playful smalldog breed, descended fromtoy breedsand spaniels. Originally from France, these charming pups are named for their butterfly-like ears. Largely popular throughout the Renaissance, were often depicted in paintings from the16th century. Although small in size, these dogs are highly active and excel in agility and obedience competitions.
papipoo: The Papipoo is a charming hybrid breed results by crossing the spiritedPapillonwith the intelligent and hypoallergenicToy Poodle. These tiny yet sturdy pups stand just 8-11 inches tall at the shoulder, with most weighing between 5-15 pounds fully grown. Their tightly curled coats take after the Poodle parent breed, coming in a variety of solid colors. With their cute butterfly ears and curly coats, Papipoos simply exude whimsy and fun. Despite their small size, these fearless pups pack huge personalities that are sure to make even the most serious dog lover laugh.
peekapoo: The Peekapoo is a small-sized designer crossbreed between the ancientChinese Pekingeseand the intelligentPoodle. Like many Poodle mixes, this unique cross results in a small, adorable companion dog with a big personality and a low-shedding coat. One of the first designer crossbreeds, the Peekapoo originated in the United States in the 1950s.
pekingese: The Pekingese originated in China over 2,000 years ago, bred as lapdogs for the Chinese imperial family. Pekingese were considered to besymbols of royaltyand good luck, and only the wealthy and powerful were allowed to own them.
pembroke welsh corgi: The Pembroke Welsh Corgi is a small, sturdy herding breed from Wales, popularly known as a Corgi. With a long body and short legs, Corgis are known for their friendly and playful personality. Today, Corgis are a low-maintenance house dog and bring joy to everyone they meet.
petit basset griffon vendeen: The Petit Basset Griffon Vendéen, also known as the PBGV, is a small and spiriteddog breedloved for their unique appearance and friendly personality. Originating from the Vendée region in France, this petitehoundbelongs to the scent hound family and was initially bred for hunting small game in challenging terrains. Possessing a lively disposition and an acute sense of smell, the Petit Basset Griffon Vendéen has become a cherished companion, renowned for their amiable nature and engaging facial expressions.
pharaoh hound: The Pharaoh Hound is an ancientdog breedthat’s changed little since their development more than 5,000 years ago. They were the dogs of kings and may have hunted gazelles with pharaohs, hence their name. This loyal hunting companion later made their way to Malta, where they’re now the national dog.
pitsky: The Pitsky is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theAmerican Pit Bull TerrierandSiberian Huskydog breeds. They range in size from medium to large, they are energetic, and loyal, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
plott: The Plott Hound, a breed originating in the United States, is a skilled and dedicatedhunting dogwith a rich history. Developed by the Plott family in North Carolina, this coonhound breed is renowned for their exceptional tracking and treeing abilities, particularly in pursuit of large game such as boars and bears. With a strong and muscular build, the Plott Hound is well-suited for endurance and agility in the rugged terrains they often encounter during hunting activities. Their short, dense coat comes in various colors, with a brindle pattern being a common and distinctive trait.
pocket beagle: The Pocket Beagle is a compact Beagle variety known for their friendly demeanor and appearance which resembles a much smaller-sized Beagle. Standing around 10 inches tall at the withers, these little dogs exhibit similar distinctive characteristics to their Beagle relatives. Despite their reduced size, Pocket Beagles otherwise maintain the same playful and outgoing nature as the larger Beagle. While their hunting instincts remain due to their Beagle ancestors, Pocket Beagles are bred as companion animals.
pointer: The Pointer, also called the English Pointer, was used for several hundred years to “point” birds and small game such as rabbits. This remarkable dog breed is aversatile field dogand an exceptional familydog breed. Pointers excel in many arenas, from the field to the show ring, agility to obedience.
polish lowland sheepdog: The Polish Lowland Sheepdog is a medium-sized workingdog breedoriginally bred for herding and guarding. Clever and courageous, Polish Lowland Sheepdogs are highly protective of their flock but very sociable with people. While they excel in working roles, they’ve seamlessly adapted as companion dogs. Although rare outside their native homeland, these Polish herding dogs are gaining popularity in the United States.
pomapoo: The Pomapoo is a small-sized, hypoallergenic cross between aPomeranianand a Toy or MiniaturePoodle. With their fluffy coats, expressive eyes, and petite size, Pomapoos are irresistibly cute companions. Known for their playful and affectionate nature, these designer dogs thrive on companionship and enjoy being the center of attention. Their adaptable temperament makes them well-suited for various living situations, from apartments to larger homes, as long as they receive ample love and attention.
pomchi: The Pomchi is an ultra-portable yet playful mix of the sassyChihuahuawith the livelyPomeranian. These tiny pups pack huge personalities into their small frames, standing just 6-10 inches tall and weighing 3-12 pounds fully grown. With their petite apple-shaped heads, pointed ears, and fluffy double coats in shades like orange, cream, black and brown, Pomchis simply exude adorable charm and moxie. Despite their small size, these pups make up for it with bold, energetic demeanors inherited from both feisty parent breeds.
pomeagle: The Pomeagle is a mixed breed dog — a cross between thePomeranianandBeagledog breeds. Small, affectionate, and curious by nature, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents. Pomeagles also go by the names Beagle Pom or Pomeranian Beagle mix. Despite their unfortunate status as a designer breed, you may find these mixed breed dogs inshelters and rescues.
pomeranian: The Pomeranian is a small companion dog breed from the Spitz family whose name is tied to their origin in the Pomerania region in central Europe. Classified as part of the toy group, Pomeranian dogs are highly energetic and intelligent. While small, weighing just 3 to 7 pounds, the Pomerian descended from the larger German Spitz. Pomeranians come in various colors and patterns and require regular grooming to maintain their luxurious coat. While they make loving and loyal pets, their small and fragile build requires careful handling and supervision, especially around children and larger animals.
pomsky: The Pomsky is a small-sized designer breed resulting from a cross between theSiberian Huskyand thePomeranian, the smallest of the Spitz breeds. This adorable hybrid inherits the Husky’s striking wolf-like appearance and the Pomeranian’s compact, manageable size, creating a dog that often looks like a miniature Husky with the expressive, fluffy charm of their Pomeranian parent. Known for their playful, affectionate, and sometimes silly personalities, Pomskies have a natural knack for drawing attention and are well-suited to families, singles, and apartment living alike. Their social, energetic nature makes them ideal companions for those who enjoy a pet that’s involved in their day-to-day life.
poochon: The Poochon is a small-sized, spunky cross between theToy PoodleandBichon Frisedog breeds. Poochons go by several names, including the Bichpoo, Bichon Poo, and Bichon Poodle. Despite their unfortunate status as a designer breed, you can find these mixed-breed dogs in shelters and breed-specific rescues, so remember to adopt! Don’t shop!
poodle: The Poodle, also called the Caniche in French or Pudel in German, is a breed of water dog categorized into four groups by size: Toy, Miniature, Medium, and Standard. Though famous for their show-ring success and aristocratic appearance, Poodles are more than just breed champions. Beneath their well-groomed exteriors and dignified demeanor lies an affectionate family companion with a rich heritage.
portuguese podengo pequeno: The Portuguese Podengo Pequeno, a small and lively dogbreedoriginating from Portugal, has a charming appearance and spirited demeanor. They have a muscular build and a distinctive wedge-shaped head, with alert almond-shaped eyes and erect ears. Renowned for their historical role as a hunter, this breed possesses an independent and determined character, making them an excellent watchdog and companion.
portuguese water dog: The Portuguese Water Dog, also called Porties for short, originated in Portugal as aworking dog. They were used for a variety of tasks, including herding sheep, retrieving waterfowl, and assisting fishermen. The breed was brought to the United States in the early 1960s and has since become a popular companion dog. Medium in size, they stand around 17-23 inches tall and weigh 35-60 pounds. They have a water-resistant double coat that is long and wavy, with a soft and dense undercoat.
pug: The Pug is a small companion dog with an iconic wrinkly face, curled tail, and giant personality. A popular breed in the Toy group, they’re immediately recognizable anywhere they go. However, not everyone may know this little ancient breed boasts a rich history stretching centuries back. Originating in China during the Shang dynasty (around 1600–1046 BCE), royalty cherished these unique dogs for their endearing personality and appearance.
pugalier: The Pugalier, a mixed breed dog crossed between thePugandCavalier King Charles Spaniel, is small, adorable, and loyal. It inherits the best qualities from both parents. Pugaliers are usually called by their mixed breed name, but may also be referred to as Pug/Cavalier King Charles Spaniel mixes.
puggle: The Puggle is a hybrid cross between aBeagleand aPugthat originated in the United States in the 1980s. Puggles have become increasingly popular in recent years and are known for their friendly and playful personalities. This mixed breed typically inherits the wrinkled face and compact body of the Pug, along with the longer muzzle and floppy ears of the Beagle. Puggles are known for their friendly and affectionate nature, making them excellent companions for families and individuals alike. They often have a playful and energetic personality, thanks to the Beagle’s hunting instincts, but also enjoy cuddling and lounging around like their Pug ancestors.
puli: The Puli is a small-medium sizedherdingbreed from Hungary known for their unique corded coat. Weighing usually no more than 35 pounds, the Puli is fairly compact but sturdy and athletic. They are incredibly gifted in their herding abilities and are even capable of herding flocks of hundreds of sheep with remarkable skill and agility. Their coat may be mop-like or fluffy and comes in various colors including cream, brown, silver, black, white, and brindle. They are considered hypoallergenic and make a great choice for those with allergies.
pyrenean shepherd: The Pyrenean Shepherd is a small—to medium-sized herding dog from the Pyrenees Mountains in France, where they were bred to assist shepherds in managing flocks of sheep. They come in two coat varieties, Rough-faced and Smooth-faced, weather-resistant, and relatively easy to maintain. Despite their small size, Pyrenean Shepherds are renowned for their fearlessness and strong work ethic, excelling in various dog sports such as agility, obedience, and herding trials.
rat terrier: The Rat Terrier is a lively and versatile small dog breed known for their keen intelligence, boundless energy, and friendly disposition. Originating in the United States, this breed was initially developed for hunting small game and pest control, earning them their “Rat Terrier” moniker. They have a compact and athletic build, with a short coat in various colors and patterns. Rat Terriers are highly trainable and excel in various canine activities, including obedience, agility, and even competitive dog sports. They are affectionate and loyal to their families, making them great companions, and their vigilant nature often makes them excellent watchdogs. Despite their small size, Rat Terriers are full of spunk and make wonderful pets for active householdswilling to provide them with mental and physical stimulation.
redbone coonhound: The Redbone Coonhounddog breedis a medium-sizedhoundwho has a gorgeous red coat, the pleading expression common to hounds everywhere, and a sweet voice that sings out when they’re on the hunt. However, they also bark a lot when they’renoton the hunt, and take a lot of patience to train.
rhodesian ridgeback: The Rhodesian Ridgeback is a handsomedog breedbred in Africa to be aversatile hunterand home guardian. These days, They’re less likely to hunt lions and more likely to hunt a soft spot on the sofa after going jogging with you.
rottador: The Rottador is a mixed breed dog–a cross between theRottweilerandLabrador Retrieverdog breeds. Large, energetic, and loyal, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
rottle: The Rottle is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theRottweilerandPoodledog breeds. Playful, intelligent, and eager to please, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
rottweiler: The Rottweiler is a large working breed from Germany, where they were known as Rottweiler Metzgerhund, which means “Rottweil butchers’ dogs.” As their name might suggest, these strong dogs were used to pull meat-laden carts to market. However, the Rottweiler’s history dates even further back, with ties to the Roman Empire, where they were used as herding and guarding dogs. Today, Rottweilers are renowned for their versatility and serve as exceptional working dogs, excelling in law enforcement roles, search and rescue, and even as service dogs. Although maybe not as well known, there are multiple types of Rottweilers: American, German, and Roman. There are also long-haired Rottweilers, who showcase a beautiful, silky coat. Of course, long-haired Rotties still come in their iconic black and tan coloring.
saint berdoodle: A Saint Berdoodle is a hybrid dog breed that is a cross between aSaint Bernardand aPoodle. This mix aims to combine the gentle giant nature of the Saint Bernard with the intelligent and hypoallergenic qualities of the Poodle. The Saint Berdoodle is a large-sized dog, typically inheriting the impressive stature of the Saint Bernard parent.
saint bernard: The Saint Bernard or St. Bernard is a giant working breed from the Western Alps of Switzerland and Italy. Gentle and devoted, these dogs were historically bred by monks in the hospice of Saint Bernard to assist in search and rescue missions in the treacherous mountain passes. Considered a giant breed, Saint Bernards weigh from 120 to 180 pounds and stand 26 to 30 inches tall at the shoulder. They have a dense, double coat that provides insulation against the cold, and they come in both short and long-haired varieties. The St. Bernard temperament is easygoing, friendly, gentle, and watchful.
saluki: The Saluki is an ancient sighthound breed from the Middle East who was once considered a gift from Allah. With a quiet and dignified temperament, Salukis are medium-to-large in size with a long, slender body. Their coats are short, smooth, and silky, with feathering around the ears and tail. They can be found in various colors including cream, white, fawn, red, and black.
samoyed: The Samoyed is a medium-sized working dog breed who was once the valued companion of the Samoyede people of northwestern Siberia. Affectionately called Sammies, the highly versatile Samoyed dogs were used to hunt, haul sleds, transport goods, herd reindeer, and warm their owners by sleeping on top of them at night. Because of this working ancestry, the Samoyed temperament can sometimes be strong-willed, but they are always friendly, gentle, and devoted.
samusky: The Samusky is a mixed breed dog–a cross between theSamoyedand theSiberian Huskydog breeds. Highly intelligent, loyal, and good-natured, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
schipperke: The Schipperke is a small, lively Belgian dog breed with a unique and distinctive appearance. These dogs are known for their fox-like face, pointed ears, and thick double coat. Typically, the fur is black, but can also be chocolate or cream. They are a compact dog, especially in comparison to other Belgian dog breeds, and usually only weigh between 10 to 16 pounds. They stand around 10 to 13 inches tall at the shoulder. Their tail is typically docked, and they have a mischievous, alert expression.
schnoodle: The Schnoodle is a kid-friendly designer dog breed created by crossing a Schnauzer with a Poodle, combining the best traits of both parent breeds. This hybrid is celebrated for their intelligence, affectionate nature, and lively personality. Schnoodles come in a range of sizes—Toy, Miniature, Standard, and Giant—depending on the size of their Schnauzer and Poodle parents. Their coats are low-shedding and can showcase various colors, including black, gray, white, sable, and apricot, making them a great choice for allergy-conscious families. Active but not overly hyper, Schnoodles are loyal companions who thrive on social interaction and love spending time with their families. They are unsuitable for long periods of isolation and form strong bonds with their owners, sometimes favoring one person in the household.
scottish deerhound: The original purpose of the Scottish Deerhounddog breedwas to hunt and bring down the Scottish roe deer, a magnificent beast twice or more the dog’s size. Today, if the Scottish Deerhound were writing a personals ad, they’d say that they enjoy long walks, opportunities to run, regular meals, and a nice sofa to nap on.
scottish terrier: An independent and stubborn character, the Scottish Terrierdog breedis also quite sensitive to praise and blame. Independent, intelligent, and hilarious in their dignified seriousness, they’re a trueTerrier, which makes them an excellent watchdog.
sealyham terrier: The Sealyham Terrier, a rare Welsh small to medium-sizedterrier breed, originated in Wales as aworking dog. Sealies were frist bred tohuntotters, foxes, and badgers. Developed in the 19th century by Captain John Edwardes at Sealyham House, the breed became increasingly popular after World War I. Soon, it was associated with Hollywood stars and the British royal family. Today, these clowns of the terrier family are primarily companion dogs and a good choice for novice pet parents. These friendly, affectionate pups will get along with the whole family, whether that family lives in an apartment or a large home with a backyard.
sheepadoodle: The Sheepadoodle is a medium-to-large-sized cross between theOld English SheepdogandPoodledog breeds known for their smart, playful, and loving personalities. Sheepadoodles go by many names, including Sheep-a-poo, Sheeppoo, Sheepdoodle, Sheepdogpoo, and Sheepdog Poodle Mix. Despite their status as a designer breed, you may find these mixed breed dogs in shelters and rescues, so remember to adopt.
shepsky: The Shepsky, also known as the Gerberian Shepsky, is a hybrid dog breed that results from the crossbreeding of aGerman ShepherdandSiberian Husky. This unique combination combines the intelligence and loyalty of the German Shepherd with the striking appearance and endurance of the Siberian Husky. Shepskies are typically medium to large-sized dogs with a well-balanced build, erect ears, and a bushy tail. Their coat can vary in color and often features a mix of the parents’ coat colors.
shetland sheepdog: The Shetland Sheepdog, affectionately known as the “Sheltie,” is a small to medium-sized herding breed from Scotland. Originally bred in the Shetland Islands to herd sheep, this breed retains its strong work ethic and sharp instincts. Shelties are known for their long, dense double coats, which come in various colors such as sable, black, and blue merle, often accented with white and tan markings. Regarding temperament, Shelties possess a gentle yet energetic personality, making them popular companions for families and individuals alike. They are highly trainable and thrive in active households where they can participate in activities such as obedience, agility, or simply playing with their loved ones.
shiba inu: The Shiba Inu dog is an ancient Japanese companion breed that dates back to at least the 3rd century BC, where they were primarily used for hunting and flushing game. The breed nearly went extinct in the early 20th century but was saved by a group of Japanese breeders who dedicated themselves to preserving them. The Shiba Inu is one of Japan’s six native breeds:Akita(large), Kishu, Hokkaido, Kai, Shikoku (medium), and Shiba (small). They are also the smallest of the spitz-type dogs native to Japan.
shichon: The Shichon, also known as the Zuchon or Teddy Bear dog, is a small-sized cross between theShih Tzuand aBichon Frisedog breeds. These small, fluffy dogs are known for their affectionate, intelligent, and sociable personalities, making them a favorite among pet owners. Combining some of the best traits of their parent breeds, Shichons are celebrated for their playful demeanor and loving companionship. Despite being a designer breed, Shih Tzu Bichon mixes can often be found in shelters and rescue organizations, making adoption a viable option for prospective owners.
shih-poo: The Shih-Poo is a small-to-medium-sized companion cross between theShih TzuandToy Poodledog breeds. Thispopular Poodle mixis known for their affectionate personality, making them a great choice for families and individuals alike. Shih-Poos typically inherit a combination of the Shih Tzu’s friendly and loyal nature with the Poodle’s intelligence and hypoallergenic coat qualities.
shih tzu: The Shih Tzu is a small companion breed from Tibet believed to have been bred from thePekingeseandLhasa Apsothousands of years ago. While their name means “little lion,” there’s nothing fierce about this dog. Friendly and affectionate, they’ve made themselves comfortable on the laps of people from all walks of life since ancient times. Today, these little dogs have found their way into homes as family companions, cherished for their small size, devoted temperament, silky and low-shedding coat, and adaptability to different living environments, including apartments.
shiranian: The Shiranian is a mixed breed dog — a cross between theShih TzuandPomeranianbreeds. Small, adorable, and loyal, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents.
shollie: The Shollie is a unique cross between the vigilantGerman Shepherdand the tirelessBorder Collie, resulting in a dogbreedwho combines the best traits of bothherdinglegends. With their impressive intelligence and unwavering loyalty, the Shollie is a versatile companion who excels in various roles, from family guardian to working dog extraordinaire.
shorkie: The Shorkie, also known as the Shorkie Tzu, Yorkie Tzu, and Shih Tzu-Yorkie mix, is a cross between theShih TzuandYorkshire Terrierdog breeds. Small, fierce, and loyal, these pups inherited some of their parents’ best qualities. Standing only 6 to 14 inches tall at the shoulder and weighing no more than 15 pounds, the Shorkie is a small dog with a big heart. Thanks to both parent breeds’ feisty but loving personalities, the Shorkie temperament is also devoted, lively, spunky, and affectionate. They are also energetic and adaptable, which makes them great dogs for active individuals.
siberian husky: The Siberian Husky is a medium-sized working breed that was first bred to pull sleds across long distances in the snowy terrain of present-day Siberia. With a beautiful, thick coat that comes in many colors and markings, Siberian Huskies are often immediately recognizable. Their blue or multi-colored eyes and striking facial masks only add to the appeal of this breed, a member of the Spitz family. It is easy to see why many are drawn to the Siberian’s wolf-like looks. It’s also important to mention this athletic, intelligent breed can act independently and challenge first-time dog parents. Sadly, many of these dogs end up in shelters because they can require more patience and care than some are willing to give.
silken windhound: The Silken Windhound is a medium-sized sighthound breed from the United States. A relatively new breed that only originated in the 1980s, Silken Windhounds are renowned for their striking appearance, agility, and friendly demeanor. Standing 18 to 24 inches at the shoulder and weighing 22-55 pounds, the breed is a result of a carefully planned breeding program aimed at creating a sighthound with a balanced combination of athleticism, grace, and amiable temperament.
silky terrier: The Silky Terrier, a small and elegantdog breed, is cherished for its silky, flowing coat and lively personality. This breed, originating from Australia, boasts a striking appearance with a fine, straight coat that can come in shades of blue and tan. Silky Terriers are known for their alert and spirited nature, often described as “big dogs in small packages.” They are intelligent, quick learners, and make excellent companions for individuals and families alike. With a keen watchdog instinct, they are also social and friendly, getting along well with children and other pets. Their adaptability to bothcityand suburban living, along with their loving and vivacious temperament, makes them a delightful addition to many households.
skye terrier: The Skye Terrier is a medium-sized terrier breed from the Isle of Skye in Scotland. Originally bred to hunt and kill badgers, otters, and foxes, the breed is today appreciated as a versatile companion. They are also among the most endangered dog breeds in the United Kingdom.
sloughi: The Sloughi is a one-person or one-familydog breedwho’s affectionate with their own people but aloof toward strangers. Like many hounds, their stubborn nature can make them too much of a handful for first-time dog parents.
small munsterlander pointer: The Small Munsterlander Pointer originated in Germany during the late 19th century through careful breeding efforts. By crossing local German Pointers with otherproficient hunting breeds, this dog was refined into a versatile tracker and retriever, well-suited to various terrains. The German Kennel Club officially recognized the breed in 1922. The Small Munsterlander later made their way to North America in the latter part of the 20th century.
soft coated wheaten terrier: The Soft Coated Wheaten Terrier is a medium-sized terrier breed from Ireland dog whose coat comes in two types: Irish and Heavy. While the Heavy, or American coat, is thicker, the Irish coat is silky and wavy. While most terriers are known for their tenacity and upbeat energy, the Wheaten is fairly easygoing. They require a moderate amount of exercise and make great family dogs. Often referred to simply as the “Wheaten,” Wheaten Terriers were originally bred as versatile farm dogs, serving as all-around working dogs on Irish farms.
spanish mastiff: The Spanish Mastiff, or the Mastín Español, is an extra-large-sized working dog with a long history of guarding sheep and livestock. While formidable in appearance, they are known for their protective, loving, and calm temperament. One of the largest dog breeds in the world, Spanish Mastiffs are gentle giants that have become popular guard dogs and family pets alike. Throughout history, Spanish Mastiffs have gone by several names, including Mastín Español and Mastín de España. While uncommon, you can still find them in shelters and breed-specific rescues.
spinone italiano: The Spinone Italiano, also known as the Italian Wirehaired Pointer, is a medium-to-large-sized gundog from Italy originally bred for hunting, tracking, and pointing. While excellent hunting dogs, many spend their time as companion animals. Loyal, friendly, and athletic, this dog would be great for an active family on the go. The Spinone Italiano (the plural form is Spinoni Italiani) goes by many names, such as Italian Spinone, Spinone, Italian Griffon, and Italian Coarsehaired Pointer. Despite their purebred status, you can find them in shelters and rescues, so always prioritize adoption.
springador: The Springador is a medium-to-large-sized hybrid dog breed resulting from a cross between aSpringer Spanieland aLabrador Retriever. This energetic and intelligent mix combines some of the best traits of its parent breeds, making it a popular choice for active families and individuals. Springadors typically have a sturdy build, with a short to medium-length coat that is often water-resistant, reflecting their working-dog heritage. Their coats can come in various colors, such as black, brown, or yellow, often with white markings. They are known for their expressive eyes and friendly demeanor, which make them both attractive and approachable.
stabyhoun: Originating from the Netherlands, the Stabyhoun dog is a medium-sizeddog breedcelebrated for their versatility and amiable nature. Recognized for their contribution as a skilled hunter and retriever, the Stabyhoun dog also makes a great devoted family companion. They have a distinctive coat of various colors and a friendly disposition, and are well-suited for both work and play.
staffordshire bull terrier: The Staffordshire Bull Terrier also called the Staffy or Stafford for short, is a small to medium-sized terrier breed from the United Kingdom. Although developed in the 19th century to be small, fast, fighting dogs, those days are long past. Sadly, this reputation still lingers, and these highly affectionate dogs face much stigma. Even though these are purebred dogs, you will often find them in the care of shelters and rescue groups.
standard schnauzer: The Standard Schnauzer was originally bred for ratting, guarding, and working on family farms in Germany. Their versatility, medium size, protective nature, and love of family also make them an excellent companiondog breed. Although they can be somewhat territorial and stubborn, dogs of this breed also have high intelligence and energy. If you’re an experienced dog parent looking for a guardian and best friend to go with you on adventures, this may be the breed for you!
sussex spaniel: The Sussex Spaniel is a medium-sized sporting dog breed from Sussex County, England bred to flush birds into the air for hunters. With a dark or golden-liver coat, Sussex Spaniels look similar in appearance to theCocker Spanielbut sit lower to the ground. With a compact and low body, they have a reputation for being slow and calm, but they liven up when they scent birds. Because Sussex Spaniels form strong bonds with their humans, they don’t fare so well when left alone for long periods of time. As a sensitive breed, they crave affection and attention. They require firm but gentle leadership during training.
swedish vallhund: The Swedish Vallhund, often called the “Viking Dog” or “Swedish Cattle Dog,” is a unique and energetic breed originating from Sweden. This small to medium-sized dog boasts a striking resemblance to a wolf and has a rich history as a versatile working dog. Vallhunds were originally used by Swedish farmers to herd cattle, control vermin, and guard their homes. Their distinctive traits include a wedge-shaped head, prick ears, a bushy tail, and a short, dense double coat that can come in various colors, with the most common being gray, sable, or red. Despite their small stature, Swedish Vallhunds are strong, agile, and well-suited for various tasks.
terripoo: The Terripoo is a cross between theAustralian Terrierand the miniaturePoodle. Intelligent, intuitive, and playful, these small dogs make excellent family pets. This energetic and playful pup inherits a delightful blend of traits from their parents. From the Australian Terrier, they gain a feisty spirit, alertness, and a love for adventure. The Miniature Poodle influence contributes intelligence, trainability, and a potentially hypoallergenic, curly coat.
texas heeler: The Texas Heeler, a vibrant mixed breed, combines the intelligence, hard work ethic, and boundless energy of theAustralian Cattle DogandAustralian Shepherdbreeds. Texas Heelers derive their name from their frequent presence in the state of Texas. This title combines with the “Heeler” nickname inherited from their Australian Cattle Dog parent, also known as the Queensland Heeler or Blue Heeler. Despite their designation as designer breeds, you can locate these mixed breed dogs in shelters and breed-specific rescues.
tibetan mastiff: Centuries ago, Tibetan Mastiffs originated in Tibet as a primitivebreed, primarily used for guarding in the tough Himalayan terrain. Today, these majestic dogs continue to fulfill their ancestral duties as guardians. They’ve also seamlessly adapted to life as cherished family companions. While once valued by farmers and herders, these dogs are now considered a luxury breed, ranking as number one forthe most expensive dog in the world.
tibetan spaniel: Small but active and alert, the Tibetan Spanieldog breedhails from mountainous Tibet, where these dogs served as a companions and watchdogs. They’re known for their intelligence, easy-care coat, and desire to keep watch over their family from high perches in the house.
tibetan terrier: The Tibetan Terrier is a medium-sized companion breed that originated as a watchdog for the Buddhist monasteries in Tibet. An ancient breed, the Tibetan Terrier has a long, double coat in various colors. Despite their name, they are not true terriers. Thebreedname was given to them by European travelers due to their resemblance to the small, fuzzy dogs of their native Europe. In Tibet, they are known as Tsang Apso or Dohki Apso, and are similar in appearance to the Lhasa Apso, although larger. The Tibetan Terrier temperament is affectionate, playful, and intelligent. They make wonderful family pets as they enjoy being with people and are fairly low-maintenance, other than their long coat which is beautiful but requires frequentgrooming.
toy fox terrier: The Toy Fox Terrier is a small-sized breed from the United States that was first developed as a smaller version of theirSmooth Fox Terrierancestor. Officially recognized as a distinct breed in 1936, this charming breed has excelled at various tasks, serving as ratters on farms and hunting small game. Their most important purpose, however, is to be loyal, loving, and devoted companions who amuse and entertain their families.
treeing tennessee brindle: The Treeing Tennessee Brindle is a distinctive and energetic breed renowned for its exceptional hunting skills and striking brindle coat. Originally developed in the United States, this breed excels at “treeing” game, meaning it can track and corner prey until the hunter arrives. With a sleek and muscular build, these hounds stand out with their unique brindle coat pattern, featuring a mix of dark stripes on a lighter background. Known for its agility and determination, this breed has a keen sense of smell, making it an excellent choice for hunting purposes.
treeing walker coonhound: The Treeing Walker Coonhound is a medium—to large-sized Hound breed descended from American and English Foxhounds. One of six Coonhound dog breeds, it was bred specifically for the treeing game. This athletic breed has an innate ability to track scents and pursue prey with remarkable agility and determination. This natural hunting instinct translates into a highly trainable and focused companion, always eager to engage in mentally stimulating activities. Their intelligence allows them to quickly grasp commands and excel in obedience training.
valley bulldog: The Valley Bulldog is a dynamic crossbreed withBoxerandEnglish Bulldogparentage. While the parent breeds are distinctly European, the Valley Bulldog’s history traces back to the Annapolis Valley of Nova Scotia, Canada, giving them the name “Valley Bulldog”. The origins of the Valley Bulldog is primarily recorded beginning in the mid-1900s, though their existence likely predates this period. While first used forworking on farms, their loyal and gentle temperament make them ideal companions for families and individuals alike. Physically, Valley Bulldogs exhibit a sturdy and muscular frame associated with the Boxer and English Bulldog breeds. With a broad chest and a muscular frame, they may immediately appear imposing. Don’t be fooled by this tough-dog exterior! They are absolute charmers, gentle, and loving. Despite their tough-dog appearance, they have an easygoing temperament and are amiable towards strangers, making them unsuitable as guard dogs but excellent as household pets. As always, socialization is key!
vizsla: The Vizsla, also known as the Hungarian Vizsla, Hungarian Pointer, or Magyar Vizsla, is a red-coated sporting breed renowned for hunting and pointing. Originating from Hungary, these hunting dogs have a rich history as versatile companions. Beloved for their athleticism and energetic nature, Vizslas do best in homes where their high exercise needs can be met. Because of their boundless energy, Vizlas require regular plenty of exercise. Additionally, adequate mental stimulation can help keep this pup happy and well-behaved.
weimaraner: The Weimaraner, also known as the “Silver Ghost,” is a medium-to-large gundog from Germany originally bred to hunt big game like deer and bear. Known for their striking silver-grey coat, the Weimaraner was a highly sought-after dog breed in their native Germany, where they originated sometime in the 19th century. Today, these elegant but demanding dogs can still be found on the hunting grounds, but they can also make fine family friends if they get enough exercise.
welsh springer spaniel: The Welsh Springer Spaniel is a medium-sized sporting dog breed that was developed to flush, or spring, game in the field. A faithful companion and full of endearing characteristics, they’re a favorite of discriminating hunters and families. The Welshie is one of Britain’s oldest Spaniel breeds, and they are a completely separate breed from the larger and more popular English Springer Spaniel. Welsh Springers have a waterproof and thornproof red and white coat that enables them to traverse over rugged terrain.
welsh terrier: The Welsh Terrier, also known as the Welshie, is a medium-sized terrier breed originally bred in the 1700s in Wales for hunting rodents, badgers, and foxes. The Welshie’s zest for life is contagious, and they always enjoy themselves to the fullest. Bred for hunting with self-determination and intelligence, the happy and lively Welshie rarely tires and desires to spend their days having fun!
west highland white terrier: The best way to describe the wee West Highland White Terrierdog breedfrom Scotland is simply to say that they’re full of self-esteem. Theydefinitelyknow they’re the best thing around. But hey, we love their confidence! Always on the lookout for a good time, they’ll make you laugh while they entertain themselves. Fans of the breed affectionately call these dogs Westies for short. And while these are fairly popular purebred dogs, you may still find them in shelters and rescues.
westiepoo: The Westipoo is a small-to-medium-sized crossbreed between aWest Highland White Terrierwith aPoodle. Sometimes called Westiedoodles and Wee-Poos, this designer breed comes in various colors and is low-shedding, making it an excellent choice for individuals with allergies. With a compact and sturdy build, these dogs are often characterized by expressive eyes and a playful demeanor.
whippet: The Whippet is a swift and gracefuldog breedknown for its speed in racing and gentle nature as a companion. Bred in England forcoursing, they have a sleek coat, slender build, and strong inclination for chasing moving objects. Despite their racing prowess, they are content with moderate exercise and enjoy short bursts of play. Their friendly disposition makes them good family pets, and they usually get along well with children and other dogs. Simple brushing and occasional baths keep their coat in good condition.
whoodle: The Whoodle is a medium-sized cross between theSoft-Coated Wheaten TerrierandPoodledog breeds. Playful, friendly, and active, thesePoodle mixesinherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents. Whoodles also go by the names the Wheatendoodle, the Wheatenpoo, the Sweatendoodle, and the Sweatenpoo. Despite their plush image, you may find these mixed-breed dogs in shelters and rescues, so remember to always adopt! Don’t shop!
wirehaired pointing griffon: The Wirehaired Pointing Griffon, also called the Korthals Griffon, is a medium-sizedsporting breedwhose breeding development begins in the Netherlands. These athletic dogs are known for their hunting abilities and are sometimes considered the supreme gundog. They are also known to have few hereditary health conditions and a long lifespan of 12 to 15 years. With their sense of humor and lively nature, they’re also a good match for an energetic family looking for a dog to be part of their daily activities.
xoloitzcuintli: The Xoloitzcuintli, also called the Mexican Hairless Dog or Xolo for short, is a hairless or coated companion dog breed who is indigenous to the land of present-day Mexico. The Aztecs domesticated these dogs, pronounced “show-low-eats-queent-lee,” making them one of only two animals they domesticated. The name “Xoloitzcuintli” is made up of two Aztec words: “Xolotl,” which means the god of fire, lightning, and death, and “itzcuintli,” which means dog. Despite the intensity of their name, Xolo dogs have a loyal and calm temperament.
yorkipoo: The Yorkipoo is a small-sized hybrid breed created by crossing aYorkshire Terrierwith a Toy or MiniaturePoodle. Often referred to as a Yorkie Poo, Yorkie-poo, or Yoodle, these small dogs typically weigh between 3 and 15 pounds, making them ideal for apartment living. Their soft, wavy coat can range from curly to straight and comes in various colors, including black, brown, cream, and white. Thanks to their Poodle lineage, many Yorkipoos have a hypoallergenic coat, which may shed less than other breeds, a bonus for allergy sufferers.
yorkshire terrier: The Yorkshire Terrier, affectionately called the Yorkie, is a small terrier breed that originated in the 19th century in Yorkshire, England, where the breed takes their name. While small in stature, standing only 8 to 9 inches tall at the shoulders and weighing no more than 7 pounds, these little dogs have huge personalities. The Yorkshire Terrier temperament is loving, spunky, independent, courageous, lively, and brave. They make great companions for people of all ages. Yorkies are also the most popular toy dog breed in the United States, and they have won many fans with their devotion to their owners, elegant looks, and suitability to apartment living.
